En: Hey, before we talk to the owner...
Zh: “喂，在我们跟这里的主人谈话之前……”

En: Not top-of-the-line glass. Hope he never has to rely on it.
Zh: “不是高配玻璃。希望他永远不要依赖这玩意。”

En: Oh... I see. That's tough, man, I feel the same way sometimes. The Man from Hjelmdall is the hero of a series of popular books based on a fictional version of Katla.
Zh: “哦……我明白了。那挺难的，伙计，我有时候也会有这种感觉。*来自赫姆达尔的男人*是一系列畅销作品里的主人公，故事是以虚构版的卡特拉创作的。”

En: You said everything's going to be over soon anyway, man. I just want to walk into the end times wearing this t-shirt.
Zh: “反正你说过，一切很快都会结束，伙计。我只想穿着这件T恤走向终结。”

En: Interesting choice. But sure, why not -- take it. Welcome to Hjelmdall.
Zh: “有趣的选择。不过当然了，为什么不可以呢——拿着它吧。欢迎来到赫姆达尔。”

En: Oh yeah?
Zh: 是吗？

En: Not now. [Leave.]
Zh: 现在不行。[离开。]

En: What a shirt. All other shirts pale in comparison to the muscular man with antlers and immense zweihänders.
Zh: 真是件好T恤。跟那个戴着鹿角，手持巨大双手剑的肌肉男相比，其他T恤瞬间显得苍白无力了。

En: Lie, sire. For no reason.
Zh: 这是谎言，大人。没有任何理由。

En: It's for the best, because Hjelmdall is *not* a real place. And thus he could not have gone there.
Zh: 这样最好，因为赫姆达尔根本就*不是*一个真实的地方。因此他也不可能去过。

En: Wow, what?
Zh: 哇哦，什么？

En: Very, *very* unique energies indeed. Geomagnetic ley lines, one might even say.
Zh: 确实是非常，*非常*独特的能量。甚至可以说是，地磁方位线。

En: The antlers on the hood of the man's cloak and his piercing blue eyes are familiar....
Zh: 男人斗篷兜帽上的鹿角，还有那双浅蓝色的眼睛看起来很熟悉……

En: Stop talking about this nerd stuff. It will make you *weedy*.  Buy the damn shirt already, it's powerful.
Zh: 别再说些傻里傻气的话了，这样会让你变得*弱不禁风*的。赶快买下那件该死的T恤吧，它很强大。

En: Stop talking about this nerd stuff. It will make you *weedy*.
Zh: 别再说些傻里傻气的话了，这样会让你变得*弱不禁风*的。

En: Couldn't you just give it to me for free then?
Zh: “你就不能免费送我吗？”

En: Wow! A very large red T-shirt with an impressive print stands out from the other garb.
Zh: 哇哦！一件非常大的红色T恤，上面的印花给人留下了深刻的印象，一下就从其他衣服里脱颖而出。

En: What's the deal with the man on this t-shirt?
Zh: “T恤上这个男人是怎么回事？”

En: That's a rad Man from Hjelmdall t-shirt you've got there.
Zh: “那是*来自赫姆达尔的男人*的T恤。”

En: How much are you selling this T-shirt for?
Zh: “这件T恤卖多少钱？”

En: Okay, I've thought it over and I want to buy the T-shirt.
Zh: “好吧，我考虑过了，我想买下这件T恤。”

En: Sniff the T-shirt.
Zh: 闻闻T恤。

En: Smells like worn cotton. And a little old sweat there?
Zh: 像是旧棉花的味道。还有一点点汗味？

En: Worn cotton with a side of flea market or trash bin.
Zh: 旧棉花，再加上一点跳蚤市场或者是垃圾桶的味道。

En: Let go of the T-shirt. [Leave.]
Zh: 放下T恤。[离开。]

En: It's the Man from Hjelmdall.
Zh: “那是来自*来自赫姆达尔的男人*。”

En: That's the Man from Hjelmdall walking away as his village burns.
Zh: “那是来自*来自赫姆达尔的男人*，他的村庄被烧毁的时候，他也离开了家乡。”

En: Hell yeah, man. The shopkeep sounds enthusiastic. "I don't usually carry printed tees, but this one was just such a pure exemplar."
Zh: “没错了，伙计。”当铺老板听起来很热情。“我一般不会拿印花T恤的，但是这一件简直就是纯粹的范例。”

En: We have a lot in common. I'm a big fan of the Man from Hjelmdall too.
Zh: “我们有很多相似之处。我也是*来自赫姆达尔的男人*的粉丝。”

En: A fan... No, I wouldn't go that far. But I do think the Hjelmdallermann Saga is an integral part of our shared reality.
Zh: “粉丝……不，我倒没有那么夸张。但我确实觉得赫姆达尔英雄传是我们共享现实的一个组成部分。”

En: Two reál.
Zh: “2雷亚尔。”

En: That's very cheap for a printed t-shirt that's still in pretty good shape.
Zh: 对于一件保存挺好的T恤来说，已经相当便宜了。

En: That's unusually cheap, isn't it?
Zh: “这件真的特别便宜啊，不是吗？”

En: The lieutenant raises his eyebrows. "That's dirt cheap."
Zh: 警督抬高眉毛。“确实特别便宜。”

En: It should belong to anyone who is ready to wear it.
Zh: “它应该属于任何准备穿上它的人。”

En: He frowns. "But... why?"
Zh: 他皱起眉头。“但是……为什么？”

En: The Man from Hjelmdall is a superstar, I'm a superstar -- it would be perfect!
Zh: “*来自赫姆达尔的男人*是一个超级明星，我也是个超级明星——那不是完美的吗！”

En: Perhaps I could repay you in some other way?
Zh: “也许我可以其他方法来支付？”

En: He thinks about it. "I suppose that makes sense, yes. Please, go ahead and take it -- Welcome to Hjelmdall."
Zh: 他想了想。“确实有点道理，没错。那就请拿走它吧——欢迎来到赫姆达尔。”

En: I guess I can't really think of any good reason.
Zh: “我猜我真的想不出什么好理由。”

En: Because I'm a broke cop without a cent to my name.
Zh: “因为我是个一文不名的警察，而且还身无分文。”

En: I sympathize, I do, but this is a for-profit enterprise.
Zh: “我很同情你，真的，但我这个店是要盈利的。”

En: I deal in goods, not services.
Zh: “我经营的是商品，不是服务。”

En: You'll just have to buy it, then, he replies with a dry smile. "But it's not like it's *expensive*."
Zh: “那你就更要买下它了，”他露出一个干巴巴的笑容，“而且它也不是*很贵*。”

En: *Oh* yeah. The print depicts a muscled  man striding toward you, a giant sword in each hand, encircled by burning embers. Behind him is a cluster of cabins engulfed in flames. Beneath him are the words: "HJELMDALL BURNING."
Zh: *哦*，是啊。印花描绘着一个肌肉发达的男人，正大步朝你走来。他的每只手上都握着一把巨剑，周身被燃烧的火焰包围着。他身后是一排被火焰吞噬的小屋。下面是一行字：“燃烧的赫姆达尔。”

En: This shirt celebrates a false hero, a guardian of the masculine order.
Zh: “这件T恤庆祝的是一个虚假的英雄，一个男性秩序的守护者。”

En: Sniffing is okay, says the shopkeep. "But please don't try anything on. Can't have you leaving your photon emissions in the fabric of things you're not going to buy."
Zh: “闻一闻是可以的，”老板回答到。“但是请不要试穿任何衣服。不能把你的光子辐射留在你不打算买的衣物上。”

En: You're not imagining it. Photon emissions? What is he talking about?
Zh: 这不是你的想象。光子辐射？他在说什么呢？

En: You must be a serious Man from Hjelmdall fan.
Zh: “你一定是*来自赫姆达尔的男人*的狂热粉丝。”

En: Remind me, who's the Man from Hjelmdall again?
Zh: “再提醒我一次，这个*来自赫姆达尔的男人*是谁？”

En: Walking away from his burning village, yes.
Zh: “离开他那被烧毁的村庄，是的。”

En: Their matter-of-fact tone belies their surprise at the fact that you didn't recognize the figure in the print.
Zh: 他们不带一丝感情的平淡口吻掩饰着对你不认识印花上那个人的惊讶之情。

En: He's surprised at your non-recognition.
Zh: 他对你不承认的行为表示惊讶。

En: He drums his fingers on the counter. "I doubt the real Man from Hjelmdall was as poor a role model as the one in the popular literature."
Zh: 他用手指敲击着柜台。“我怀疑真正的*来自赫姆达尔的男人*就像通俗文学里的一样，是个可怜的角色模型。”

En: Sounds like a good deal, although I wouldn't really know.
Zh: “听起来是个好主意，不过我真的不知道。”

En: Oh, it is.
Zh: “哦，是这样的。”

En: Cool, I'll think about it.
Zh: “赞，我会考虑一下。”

En: He inclines his head. "You should."
Zh: 他歪了歪头。“你应该这么做。”

En: Welcome to Hjelmdall, officer.
Zh: “欢迎来到赫姆达尔，警官。”

En: I'm guessing I'm supposed to know who this Man from Hjelmdall is, but I lost my memory recently and need help remembering basic facts about reality.
Zh: “我猜我应该知道这个*来自赫姆达尔的男人*是谁，但是我最近失忆了，需要帮助才能想起现实的基本事实。”

En: (Lie) "I was a very sheltered child -- my parents only let me read boring books about ordinary people, and the Man from Hjelmdall looks *extraordinary*."
Zh: （撒谎）“我是个过度保护的孩子——我的父母只让我阅读讲普通人的无聊书籍，这个*来自赫姆达尔的男人看起来真的很*特别*。”

En: He's a music-based superstar, right?
Zh: “他是个跟音乐有关的超级明星，对吧？”

En: That's very sad, man. Then you missed out on some pretty well executed, albeit repetitive adventure literature.
Zh: “那可真是太悲惨了，伙计。你错过了一些写得很好，但是有些千篇一律的冒险文学作品。”

En: The Man from Hjelmdall is the hero of a series of popular books based on a fictional version of Katla, mostly what is nowadays Arda NFD.
Zh: “*来自赫姆达尔的男人*是一系列畅销作品里的主人公，故事是以虚构版的卡特拉创作的，现在主要指的是阿尔达NFD。”

En: He grimaces. "I wouldn't go so far as to say that I'm a fan. But I do think the Hjelmdallermann Saga is an integral part of our shared reality."
Zh: 他做了个鬼脸。“我倒是不会说自己是个粉丝。但我确实觉得*赫姆达尔英雄传*是我们共享现实的一个组成部分。”

En: But, yes, the books are very sexist. In a not-entirely-unenjoyable way.
Zh: “但是，没错，这些书非常的男性至上主义，不过表达方式并没有完全让人感到无趣。”

En: What did you mean the Man from Hjelmdall was real?
Zh: “你说*来自赫姆达尔的男人*是真的，是什么意思？”

En: I mean -- even if the Man from Hjelmdall didn't exist before the adventure novels, the stories have made it so that he has. It's simple really.
Zh: “我的意思是——就算*来自赫姆达尔的男人*在冒险小说之前不存在，那个故事也让他变成了真实的存在。就是这么简单。”

En: Most people don't think that the Man from Hjemdall really existed, but they're *wrong*.
Zh: “大部分人都不觉得*来自赫姆达尔的男人*真的存在，但是他们*错了*。”

En: Hmm, I think you might be thinking about someone else I... dislike music, he frowns. "So I couldn't tell you who."
Zh: “嗯，我觉得你可能在想其他人……我不喜欢音乐，”他皱起眉头，“所以我也不能告诉你是谁。”

En: In fact... He leans in. "Most people don't think that the Man from Hjelmdall ever really existed, but they are *wrong*."
Zh: “其实呢……”他靠了过去。“大部分人都不觉得*来自赫姆达尔的男人*真的存在，但是他们*错了*。”

En: The lieutenant raises a brow. "*Hjelmdall* isn't a real place."
Zh: 警督抬高眉毛。“*赫姆达尔*都不是一个真实的地方。”

En: Neither is the Man a real man, of course, but both the Man and Hjelmdall are an ontological necessity... He stops abruptly. "But, hey, it's not worth getting into an argument about."
Zh: “当然了，那个人也不是真实存在的人，但是那个男人和赫姆达尔是存在论的必需品……”他突然停了下来。“不过，我们也没必要为了这个而争论。”

En: He left his native village of Hjelmdall after his son was kidnapped, having many exciting adventures and killing many enemies with his swords, but he lost his family in the process. Tragic stuff, really.
Zh: “他的儿子被绑架之后，他就离开了生养他的赫姆达尔村庄，踏上了精彩万分的冒险旅途，用手中的宝剑斩杀了很多敌人，不过中途他失去了自己的家人。真的挺惨烈的。”

En: Okay... He sounds incredulous.
Zh: “好吧……”他听起来有些怀疑。

En:
Uh huh, say more?'	“嗯哼，再多说点？”
0x0100005800001397	Dialogue Text	PAWNSHOP / RACK OF SECOND-HAND UNIFORMS	You	I'm totally with you, man. Reality has a way of bending back on itself."
Zh: “我完全同意你的说法，伙计。现实有一种让自己首尾相接的方式。”

En: You sound sceptical. It's not that complicated. All that's required is a more robust understanding of cause and effect.
Zh: “你好像很怀疑。其实没那么复杂。唯一需要的就是加深对因果关系的理解而已。”

En: It's simply a matter of recognizing that cause and effect work in both directions.
Zh: “其实只是个简单的认知问题：因果关系是双向的。”

En: Exactly. Cause and effect work in both directions.
Zh: “没错。因果关系是双向的。”

En: It's not that complicated. All that's required is a more robust understanding of cause and effect.
Zh: “其实没那么复杂。唯一需要的就是加深对因果关系的理解而已。”

En: Besides, I've been to Katla, though not quite as far north as the Hjelmdall, and watched the northern lights travel across the sky. Very unique energetic tides there.
Zh: “再说了，我去过卡特拉——不过不像赫姆达尔那么偏北，看过极光划过天空。那里有非常独特的能量潮汐。”

En: I'm not following at all, sorry.
Zh: “抱歉，我完全跟不上。”

En: I'm not following at all.
Zh: “我完全跟不上。”

En: I'm going to call bullshit right now.
Zh: “我现在要叫停这些鬼话了。”

En: He smirks. "It's a simple matter of cause and effect, and recognizing that they work in both directions."
Zh: 他得意地笑了。“只是简单的因果问题，只要认识到它们是双向的就可以。”

En: His theory isn't exactly *incoherent*, but its logic does suggest some *unusual* neural activity. Interesting...
Zh: 他的理论并没有完全的*不合逻辑*，不过也确实暗示了某种*不寻常*的神经活动。有点意思……

En: Huh?
I said I lost my memory recently and need help remembering basic facts about reality.
Zh: “我说我最近失忆了，需要帮助才能想起现实的基本事实。”

En: Never mind. Tell me more about this Man from Hjelmdall.
Zh: “没关系。再跟我多说说这个*来自赫姆达尔的男人*。”

En: Entertained? He might be high -- if he is, on what?
Zh: 高兴？他可能是嗨了吧——如果是的话，到底是什么呢？

En: Looking at his wares, talking to him -- that might give you more clues.
Zh: 看看他的商品，跟他聊聊——这样也许能给你更多线索。

En: A guarded man like him wouldn't tell you if you asked out loud.
Zh: 一个像他这样谨慎的人，如果你大声问出来，他是什么也不会说的。

En: Pyrholidon? What is that?
Zh: 吡嚯浣酮？那是什么？

En: A drug developed by the military to treat and prevent radiation sickness. It has psychedelic side-effects. *And* it makes your eyes turn yellow!
Zh: 军方开发的一种药物，用来治疗和防治辐射病的，有致幻的副作用。*而且*会让你的眼睛变黄！

En: A bad idea. Some poor leftists built a particle decay generator in hopes of bringing affordable electricity to under-served communities. It malfunctioned. Radioactive waste everywhere, probably some of it in *you*, too.
Zh: “一个坏主意。有些可怜的左翼分子建造了一台粒子衰变发电机，希望能为服务水平低下的社区提供价格实惠的电力。它发生故障了。辐射废料到处都是，也许*你*身体里也有。”

En: There wasn't much the volunteer force could do, however. We wasted years in the river mud. Years getting sick... He looks at the spiralling light and stops.
Zh: “但是，志愿部队也做不了多少。我们在泥泞的河水里浪费了好多年。染上一身的病……”他望着旋转摇曳的灯光，停了下来

En: So much bitterness.
Zh: 有太多苦痛。

En: ...go ahead then, he thinks. As long as you can walk straight, I don't care.
Zh: ……那就继续吧，他想到。只要你还能走直线，我就不在乎。

En: He won't be your narc -- but he won't be thrilled about this either.
Zh: 他不是你的缉毒刑警——但他也不会为此感到兴奋。

En: A bunch of poor people built themselves a primitive nuclear reactor, hoping for the best. What do you think is going to happen?
Zh: “一群可怜虫给自己建了个简陋的核反应堆，期盼最美好的结果。你觉得会发生什么？”

En: Gone forever...
Zh: 永远地离去了……

En: The rest is for him -- to compensate for the pain of being separated from his radiant spinners.
Zh: 剩下的都是给他的——为了补偿与他那光芒四射的轮圈分离的痛苦。

En: The windfall is a small consolation.
Zh: 这笔意外之财只是小小的慰藉。

En: Yeah... you gotta get in on those vortices, my man.
Zh: 是啊……你得加入这些漩涡，我的朋友。

En: Actually, no -- I don't have the money.
Zh: “其实呢，不用了——我没有钱。”

En: Your choice, officer. I can't promise I can hold on to it for much longer, however.
Zh: “这是你的选择，警官。不过，我不敢保证能帮你继续留着。”

En: He doesn't know anything.
Zh: 他什么也不知道。

En: No one likes to see what you have to see every day.
Zh: 没人想看见你每天看见的东西。

En: You have absolutely no idea what *aphotic paths* are, but the tattoos on the man are not that.
Zh: 你完全不知道*无光路径*是什么，不过男人身上的纹身绝对不是。

En: WHAT? *Sold*?!
Zh: 什么？*卖了*？！

En: This is a pawn shop... And it *did* feel as if you've met before. Oh god...
Zh: 这里是当铺……而且感觉你们之前*确实*见过。天啦……

En: Your *mother* is a necrotic object. I'm fun. Look at me sparkle in the light of the projector!
Zh: 你的*母亲*是一个坏死的物体。我很有趣。看看我，在这个投影仪的光芒下闪闪发光！

En: You can sell things from your ITEMS tab for cash -- but be careful: You might wish you had them later.
Zh: 你可以售卖物品栏中的物品以获取现金。但注意：你可能以后会用到它们。

En: On the patch -- gamma radiation lines crossed with a red drop of blood.
Zh: 在那个补丁上——伽马辐射线与一滴红色的血液交叉着。

En: Tough son of a gun, this one. Respect.
Zh: 这家伙真的很强悍。我尊重他。

En: I haven't -- but don't worry, I can take it.
Zh: “我没有——不过别担心，我可以接受。”

En: Very *normal* behaviour. You must've been in a great shape.
Zh: 非常*普通的*行为。你的身体状况一定很好。

En: This has got to be the most.... wow...
Zh: 那一定是最……哇哦……

En: There's *pity* there too. In case you didn't notice.
Zh: 这里也有点*遗憾*。以防你没有注意到的话。

En: A needle in a haystack. There is nothing you can do it about it now. You'll just have to hope you luck upon her somehow.
Zh: 大海捞针。现在你没什么可以做的。你只能寄希望于她的好运了。

En: Of course.
Zh: “当然。”

En: A needle in a haystack. There is nothing you can do about it right now. You'll just have to hope Evrart wasn't lying and the Union can track her down in time.
Zh: 大海捞针。没什么可以做的。你只能希望艾弗拉特没有撒谎，而且工会能及时追踪到她。

En: Not very. I need to funk it up.
Zh: “不算很好。我需要让它嗨起来。”

En: You're quite invested in this. He looks at the bundle of tape in front of him. It shimmers under the shop's dazzling light show.
Zh: “你对它期望挺大的。”他看着面前的那一堆磁带。它在店铺绚烂的灯光秀下闪闪发光。

En: I'm back for the transceiver. Is it still available?
Zh: “我回来拿收发器了。还有多的吗？”

En: I'm looking for a radio transceiver. You got anything?
Zh: “我在找无线电收发器。你这儿有吗？”

En: A transceiver, is it? Yeah, we should have something to do the trick...
Zh: “收发器吗？嗯，我们应该有这玩意儿……”

En: For the moment, yes.
Zh: “目前还有。”

En: He produces a machine encased in a blue bakelite shell, its black face covered in an assortment of pearl-topped knobs and dials.
Zh: 他拿出了一台用蓝色胶木外壳包裹的机器，机器的黑色表面上布满了各种旋钮和刻度盘。

En: A Kristallsprach Model 9. Gottwaldian engineering at its finest. This is a specialist's model, ideal for picking up foreign signals...
Zh: “这是晶体斯普拉赫Model 9。戈特伍德的工程学的集大成之作。这一款是专业型号，非常适合用来接收国外信号……”

En: The lieutenant whistles a long, quivering note...
Zh: 警督吹了一个长长的、颤抖的口哨……

En: A genuine Kristallsprach. This is a connoisseur's model. Good for picking up foreign milieus.
Zh: “真是晶体斯普拉赫啊。这款是内行人用的。很适合用来接收国外信号。”

En: The Model 9 also comes equipped with a signal purifier to suppress crosstalk, especially good around here with our funky reception.
Zh: “9型还配置了一个信号过滤器，可以用来防止串扰，特别适合我们这种接待工作。”

En: In other words, it'll put even the lieutenant's beloved DynaWave to shame.
Zh: 换句话讲，它甚至会让警督心爱的动力波都自惭形愧。

En: Sounds perfect!
Zh: “感觉太棒了！”

En: Sounds a bit like overkill.
Zh: “感觉有些过了。”

En: Sounds expensive.
Zh: “感觉很贵。”

En: It's about as close to perfection as a transceiver gets. And we're only asking 120 reál for it.
Zh: “它几乎能够达到收发器功能的极限。而我们只要120雷亚尔。”

En: Could be, if you're not concerned about quality of signal. But real audiophiles will pay good money for this. That's why we can't let it go for less than 120 reál.
Zh: “如果你不在乎信号质量的话，那确实是。但是真正的发烧友愿意为它破费。所以我们要价不能低于120雷亚尔。”

En: Well it's not that Iilmaraan surplus junk, that's for sure. We think 120 reál is more than reasonable.
Zh: “这可不是伊尔玛的垃圾玩意儿。我们觉得120雷亚尔的价格很公道。”

En: Buy the highly expensive transceiver.
Zh: 买下这个昂贵的收发器。

En: I'm gonna pass after all. Thanks again, Roy.
Zh: “那还是算了吧，谢了，罗伊。”

En: I'm gonna shop around a bit. I might be back later.
Zh: “我再去附近逛逛吧，回头再来。”

En: You won't regret it, officer. Whatever you need, the Model 9 can handle.
Zh: “你买了绝对不会后悔，警官。Model 9能满足你的一切需求。”

En: You'll be back again, I've got a feeling. He returns the transceiver to its shelf once more.
Zh: “我觉得你还会回来的。”他重新把收发器放回架子上。

En: Suit yourself. Can't promise this beauty'll be here when you come back, though. He gently returns the transceiver to its shelf behind the counter.
Zh: “那随便你吧。但我可不保证你回来的时候这个宝贝还在。”他轻轻地把收发器放回柜台后面的架子上。

En: The lieutenant watches you take the transceiver with barely disguised envy.
Zh: 警督见你拿着收发器，眼神里露出妒忌。

En: You jealous, Kim?
Zh: “你是在嫉妒吗，金？”

En: Can't wait to take this baby for a spin.
Zh: “等不及想要用这个宝贝了。”

En: Not at all, detective. A Kristallsprach is a fine piece of equipment, but I'm quite satisfied with my DynaWave.
Zh: “完全没有，警探。晶体斯普拉赫虽然相当不错，但我自己的动力波也足够了。”

En: Of course, you need a certain level of *discernment* to really appreciate the difference in quality.
Zh: “当然，你得有那个*眼力*才能真正区分出质量差别。”

En: Oh, he's *totally* jealous. He can barely hold it in!
Zh: 哈，他*绝对是*嫉妒了。他藏不住了！

En: He's reaching for naked snobbery because he knows you've got him *beat* from a technical angle.
Zh: 他之所以摆架子，是因为他知道你在技术层面上*击败*了他。

En: Do you think we'll actually be able to reach the Coalition with this?
Zh: “你真觉得我们能用这个联系到联盟吗？”

En: Thanks, Roy, this is just what I needed. (Conclude.)
Zh: “谢了，罗伊，我正需要这个。”（总结。）

En: We're glad to hear it. He smiles as the reflected light dances across his lenses.
Zh: “那就好。”他微笑着答道，眼镜上闪过一道光芒。

En: I wouldn't like to guess. Your performance so far doesn't exactly inspire confidence, but all sorts of things are possible...
Zh: “不好说。你目前的表现让人实在提不起信心，但一切皆有可能吧……”

En: *You* could reach the Coalition and have your talk about *la responsabilité*... *I* could grow 10 centimetres and become a flanker for the Stormers.
Zh: “*你*可能会到联盟去宣扬你的所谓*责任*……而*我*也可能会再长高10厘米，成为突击队的侧卫……”

En: Who's to say what's possible and what's not?
Zh: “什么事有可能，什么事没可能，这谁说得清呢？”

En: Do you recognise net worth in here? I have a *lot* of it. (Show him the certificate.)
Zh: “你觉得这个值多少钱？这个我有*很多*呢。”（给他看看股票。）

En: He scans the document in your hand. "This is--" He's interrupted by a sudden cough. "I certainly wouldn't pass up the opportunity to acquire something that can make princes out of peons."
Zh: 他看了看你手中的东西。“这是——”一阵咳嗽声将他的话语打断。“从奴仆变成王子的机会摆在我面前，我当然不会错过。”

En: Owning shares -- that's where the proper trade is. Far beyond the pittance I deal with in here.
Zh: “股票啊——那才是真的交易呢。那赚得比我的微薄收入可高多了。”

En: What's wrong with your shop? I like it in here.
Zh: “你的店有什么问题吗？我还挺喜欢这儿的。”

En: What can I trade for these shares then? *Everything*? (Proceed.)
Zh: “这些股票能用来换什么东西呢？这儿的*一切*吗？”（继续。）

En: Nothing's *wrong* with it. The shop suits me fine. It's just... He changes tack. "We all need a bit of luck in our lives, you know? A lottery win."
Zh: “这儿没什么*问题*。这家店也很适合我。只不过……”他话锋一转。“人生总是需要一点运气的，你懂吗？需要中个大奖什么的。”

En: In theory, yes, you probably have enough to afford it all. However, I don't plan on selling up and joining the mastodons of La Delta just yet.
Zh: “理论上来说，大概真能把这里全买下。可我现在还不打算把店清空，然后加入德尔塔的上流社会。”

En: Shades here is certainly junked up enough to fit in with the big dicks swinging wild and free across the river.
Zh: 这儿的垃圾和这家店非常搭调，就像在河里肆意穿行的大阳具一样。

En: What I can do is trade that modified street light over there for some of your shares. It's the most expensive item in here -- the finest in interior lighting.
Zh: “我只能用那边那盏改装过的路灯来换你一部分股票。那是店里最贵的东西了——能提供最棒的室内照明。”

En: Yeah, okay, that seems like it has potential.
Zh: “哦，好吧，这东西看起来还不错。”

En: I'm not sure. I'll certainly think about it.
Zh: “我没下定主意。不过我会考虑看看的。”

En: Have another look at it. Really take in that quality craftsmanship. Any other questions in the meantime?
Zh: “你再看看吧。好好感受这工艺有多么精湛。对了，你还有别的事情吗？”

En: Take a closer look. You'll see the quality of the craftsmanship then. Any other questions in the meantime?
Zh: “仔细看看吧，你就会明白这工艺是多么精湛了。对了，你还有别的事情吗？”

En: He's hiding something.
Zh: 他有所隐瞒。

En: Onwards and upwards. (Give him a thumbs up.)
Zh: “没问题。”（朝他竖起大拇指。）

En: Something like that, officer.
Zh: “差不多吧，警官。”

En: Is there something you're not telling me?
Zh: “你有什么事情没告诉我吗？”

En: Let him hide.
Zh: 让他隐瞒下去。

En: He lets out a long sigh. "I'm in debt, officer. I've felt *stuck* for a long time." He looks at the paper in your hand. "Those stocks -- they could be my way out."
Zh: 他长叹一声。“我欠着债呢，警官。我已经*一贫如洗*很久了。”他看向了你手中的那叠股票。“这些股票……也许能救我的急。”

En: You're selling it for 700 reál, right?
Zh: “这东西你卖700雷亚尔，对吗？”

En: Right. He nods.
Zh: “是的。”他点点头。

En: The pain written in the creases of his face... sheesh! This is a man who's been trying to catch falling knives for *years*.
Zh: 他布满皱纹的脸上露出了痛苦的神情……哎呀！这家伙*这些年*一直在抄底。

En: The bric-à-brac man looks at you from behind two layers of polished glass. It is nearly impossible to read any emotion. He sways slightly and itches his cheek.
Zh: 这个贫穷的男人透过眼镜看向你。你很难看穿他的心思。他微微摇晃着身子，挠了挠脸颊。

En: The bric-à-brac man looks at you from behind two layers of polished glass. It is nearly impossible to read any emotion. He sways slightly and itches his cheek.
Zh: 这个贫穷的男人透过眼镜看向你。你很难看穿他的心思。他微微摇晃着身子，挠了挠脸颊。

En: He hides his struggle well, just like he does the scarred walls of his shop -- concealed behind stacks of dusty merchandise.
Zh: 他隐藏起心中的挣扎，就像把店里陈旧破损的墙面隐藏在一堆灰尘满满的商品背后那样。

En: If there is any struggle in him, he hides it well, just like he does the scarred walls of his shop -- concealed behind stacks of dusty merchandise.
Zh: 如果他的心中有挣扎，那他一定隐藏得很好，就像把店里陈旧破损的墙面隐藏在一堆灰尘满满的商品背后那样。

En: Expensive isn't everything. And we know that light isn't even a recognisable *brand*. You can't exactly strut down the street and turn heads with it like you can a new Perseus Black number, either.
Zh: 贵的不一定好。而且这盏灯估计连*牌子*都没有。把这东西放在大街上估计都不如穿套新的珀尔修斯·布莱克制服更引人注目。

En: Expensive isn't everything. Bet this light doesn't even have a recognisable *brand*. And you can't exactly strut down the street and turn heads with it like you can a new Perseus Black number.
Zh: 贵的不一定好。这盏灯估计连*牌子*都没有。把这东西放在大街上估计都不如穿套新的珀尔修斯·布莱克制服更引人注目。

En: Good point. I won't bother with it then.
Zh: 行吧，那我就不用纠结了。

En: It's still a physical asset, though. Doesn't that count for anything?
Zh: 但它好歹算物质财产。这样也不行吗？

En: Let's not *completely* write it off yet. Perhaps there's a business venture to be had here. Any ideas?
Zh: 先别*全盘*否定吧。也许能当成是一次投资，不是吗？

En: Turn it into an art installation. Invite viewers to question what it means to bring an outside light, *inside*? We have so many barriers to break down and concepts to explore... for a price, of course.
Zh: 就把它当成艺术装置吧。我们可以邀请观众来欣赏，询问他们把室外的灯放到*室内*有什么含义？我们要突破的障碍，要探索的理念还有许多……当然，这一切都是有成本的。

En: Sure it does. Not saying you should definitely *not* get it. But let's think about what to do with it first. Any ideas?
Zh: 确实如此。并不是说你绝对*不该*买。但是我们应该先想想它有什么用，不是吗？

En: Imagine swinging it at a ball -- the distance you'd get! That's the kind of feat that nets sportsmen a corporate sponsorship.
Zh: 想象一下用它来打球的感觉——这能打多远啊！能取得如此战绩的网球运动员绝对能获得大公司的赞助。

En: There's much to be learned in this kind of rewiring job. You could steal its secrets and make a whole line of modified street tech to sell to the public.
Zh: 这东西在重新配线方面有很多值得学习的地方。你可以窃取其中的秘密，打造出一整条街的科技改装生产线，然后卖给公众。

En: It's been through a whole *transformation*. There are so many insights to share -- it just needs a medium. Wouldn't the wake-walkers pay to hear the somniloquy of the spirit light?
Zh: 它经历过一场完整的*转变*。这其中可以分享的想法太多了——而我们需要的只是一种媒介。那些浑浑噩噩度日的人们难道不该花钱去听听看灵性之光的梦呓吗？

En: It's up to you. If you think it's got ROI potential then go ahead. Just bear in mind that these shares are developing into a fortune without you having to do anything.
Zh: 你自己决定吧。如果你觉得它的投资回报率还不错，那就买吧。不过记好了，你什么都不用做，这些股票都能升值。

En: And you won't give me anything else for these shares?
Zh: “这些股票换不了别的东西吗？”

En: Nothing else in here carries that much value, I'm afraid. Plus, I need to make sure I still earn *some* actual cash -- for food and such.
Zh: “店里恐怕没有更值钱的东西了。再说，我也得保证自己还有商品可以卖出*一点*现金——用来买食物什么的。”

En: You want shares off me? I was hoping to get a discount or some freebies.
Zh: “你要这些股票吗？希望你能给我打个折，或者送我一些赠品。”

En: No can do. I need to make money -- this is a business first and foremost. Not a charity.
Zh: “不行，我得赚钱啊——这儿可是做生意的地方，不是慈善机构。”

En: Of course. I'd be glad to trade for your shares. Let's stick to that.
Zh: “当然，我很乐意收下你的股票。就这么决定了。”

En: What if I endorse your store? Put my official stamp of approval on it?
Zh: “我帮你的店背书，然后在上面盖上我的公章怎么样？”

En: I understand. It was worth a shot.
Zh: “我明白了，可以试试。”

En: No offence but I don't think it would do me much good. Nothing to do with you -- it's just my customers tend to be, well... they wouldn't know who you are.
Zh: “无意冒犯，但这对我来说好像没什么意义。和你本人没有关系——只不过，我的客人……并不知道你是谁。”

En: He's right. His customers are desperates, lowlifes, and bored browsers. They don't understand *high culture*. They don't want their purchases to elevate them. They just want some old junk at a bargain price.
Zh: 他说得对。他的顾客都是些亡命之徒、底层人民，还有街溜子。他们自然不懂什么是*阳春白雪*。他们根本不会考虑去提高自己的生活品质。他们想要的就只是廉价的废旧垃圾。

En: I'd be glad to trade for your shares. Let's stick to that.
Zh: “我很乐意收下你的股票。就这么决定了。”

En: He seems down on himself. Offer a bit of positivity and you might put him in a more benevolent mood.
Zh: 他好像有些低落。给他点甜头，或许能让他的态度更友善些。

En: That didn't work? He must have sunk pretty low.
Zh: 这样都不管用？他一定是相当沉沦了。

En: How did you get into debt?
Zh: “你是怎么背上债务的？”

En: That must have taken its toll on you.
Zh: “你肯定损失惨重吧。”

En: After I acquired the shop I had a bit of extra cash on my hands. Could do anything I wanted with it. So what *does* a young man do in that situation?
Zh: “我买下这间店铺过后手里还剩了点钱，可以用来做点自己想做的事情。在这种情况下，一个年轻人通常会*怎么做*呢？”

En: Yeah. I always thought it would be the toxic exposure that killed me. Never realised how haggard years and years of stress can make you. But that's why it's one of the biggest killers in the world, I guess.
Zh: “是啊。我一直以为是接触了有毒物质才害了自己。从没想过长年累月的压力竟然会把人摧残成这样。所以人们才说压力是世界上最大的杀手。”

En: Yeah. It's surprising how haggard years and years of stress can make you. But that's why it's one of the biggest killers in the world, I guess.
Zh: “是啊，长年累月的压力竟然会把人摧残成这样。所以人们才说压力是世界上最大的杀手。”

En: I sure as hell didn't know. All I could think to do was pump it into the relief effort. I had to do *something* after I left the frontlines... for them, but also for myself.
Zh: “那时候我怎么知道。我只知道把钱都拿去赈灾。离开前线之后，我觉得自己必须做点*什么*……不仅是为了他们，也为了我自己。”

En: I sure as hell didn't know. So I pumped it into charities to help the less fortunate. I had to do something... for them, but also for myself.
Zh: “那时候我怎么知道。所以才会把钱都捐给慈善机构，帮助那些不幸的人。离开前线之后，我觉得自己必须做点*什么*……不仅是为了他们，也为了我自己。”

En: What *doesn't* he do?
Zh: 他有什么是*不做的*？

En: That doesn't explain how you got into *debt*...
Zh: “这还是不能说明你是怎么*负债*的……”

En: He was already scarred...
Zh: 他已经历尽沧桑了……

En: Mauled by a nightmare. Bite marks in his bones.
Zh: 他终日被梦魇折磨，钻心刻骨。

En: Something just shuddered up his spine.
Zh: 他的脊梁骨都颤抖了起来。

En: Did it help?
Zh: “那样有用吗？”

En: No... I soon started searching for other ways to cope with the mental trauma. Being cooped up alone most of the day was exacerbating it -- just my own head to consort with.
Zh: “没有……不久之后，我就开始寻找别的方法来治疗精神创伤。大部分时间我都是一个人待着，自己胡思乱想——情况也因此恶化了。”

En: For a little while. But I soon started searching for other ways to cope with the mental trauma. Being cooped up alone most of the day was exacerbating it... just my own head to consort with.
Zh: “缓解了一阵子吧。可不久之后，我就开始寻找别的方法来治疗精神创伤。大部分时间我都是一个人待着，自己胡思乱想——情况也因此恶化了。”

En: No... Being cooped up alone most of the day wasn't healthy for me -- just my own head to consort with. But a friend of mine knew of a way to deal with that.
Zh: “没有……整天一个人待着胡思乱想，对我的健康没有一点好处。但我有个朋友倒是有办法。”

En: For a little while. Being cooped up alone most of the day wasn't healthy for me... just my own head to consort with. But a friend of mine knew of a way to deal with that.
Zh: “缓解了一阵子吧。整天一个人待着胡思乱想，对我的健康没有一点好处。但我有个朋友倒是有办法。”

En: It helped but it was another monthly outgoing... still is. It would have been fine but I was naive back then. Didn't think I'd ever run out of money...
Zh: “有用是有用，但是那又是另一笔日常开销了……直到现在也是。我当时太天真了，根本没想过自己会把钱花光……”

En: A friend of mine knew of a way to deal with that. That's when I started riding the pyro, as they say. It helped -- a lot, actually. But it was another monthly outgoing... still is.
Zh: “我有个朋友倒是有办法。所以从那个时候起，我开始接触*吡咯*。倒是很有效——非常有效。可是那又是另一笔日常花销了……直到现在也是。”

En: It helped but it was another monthly outgoing... still is. It would have been fine but I was naive back then. Didn't think I'd ever run out of money...
Zh: “有用是有用，但是那又是另一笔日常花销了……直到现在也是，可我当时太天真了。我根本没想过自己会把钱花光……”

En: A friend of mine knew of a way to deal with that. That's when I started riding the pyro, as they say. That did help -- a lot, actually. But it was another monthly outgoing... still is.
Zh: “我有个朋友倒是有办法。所以从那个时候起，我开始接触*吡咯*。倒是很有效——非常有效。可是那又是另一笔日常花销了……直到现在也是。”

En: This debt -- is it the drugs?
Zh: “所以你负债——是因为毒品吗？”

En: Yes and no. It started when I had a bit of extra cash on me after acquiring the shop. Could do anything I wanted with it. So what *does* a young man do in that situation?
Zh: “是，也不是。我买下这间店铺过后手里还剩了点钱，可以用来做点自己想做的事情。在这种情况下，一个年轻人通常会*怎么做*呢？”

En: And now he needs it just to exist...
Zh: 现在这东西成了他维持生命的必需品了……

En: Yeah. But that's enough of my sob story. Let's get back to business, he says, as if this conversation has changed nothing between you.
Zh: “嗯。我的悲惨故事就不再多说了。我们还是回到正题上吧，”这段交谈并没有改变你们之间的关系。

En: Man, you've really been through the wringer.
Zh: “兄弟，你受的苦可不少啊。”

En: Sounds like you've had it hard.
Zh: “你好像过得很艰苦啊。”

En: Sounds like you'll just get into debt again even if you do pay it off...
Zh: “感觉就算你现在还清了债务，以后还是会再次负债的……”

En: No chance. I've got a better grip on my finances these days. This is a mistake from my younger days that just won't go away.
Zh: “不可能，我现在已经很能管住钱了。那是我年轻的时候犯下的错误，一直纠缠我到现在而已。”

En: But that's enough of my sob story. Let's get back to business, he says, as if this conversation has changed nothing between you.
Zh: “我的悲惨故事就说到这吧。我们还是回归正题，”他说，仿佛想说这段交谈并没有改变你们之间的关系。

En: I think I've tried it once -- and liked it.
Zh: “我好像之前试过一次——还挺喜欢的。”

En: The lieutenant steps away, pretending to admire some of the knick-knacks on display. Looks like he doesn't want to get involved...
Zh: 警督走向一边，假装在欣赏陈列的小物件。看来他不想掺合进来……

En: Great! Could you do it, please? This is important, I need to be able to play this tape for someone.
Zh: “太好了！请问你能帮我吗？这很重要，为了某个人，我要播放这盘磁带。”

En: Worthless? It's not worthless, Roy. This could be the next big thing for the local dance music scene.
Zh: “不值钱？它不是一文不值的，罗伊。这可能是本地舞曲里的下一个爆款。”

En: Huh? He slowly taps his fingers on the counter. "What do you mean?"
Zh: “哈？”他慢慢用手指拍打着柜台。“你什么意思？”

En: I met some young enthusiasts down the coast. And they play this weird dance music... which they call *anodic music*.
Zh: “我在海岸边遇到了一群年轻的狂热分子。他们在播放一种古怪的舞曲……好像叫什么*阳极音乐*。”

En: I met some young enthusiasts down the coast. And they play this weird neo-disco music...
Zh: “我在海岸边遇到了一群年轻的爱好者。他们在玩一种奇怪的新迪斯科音乐……”

En:
No, that's the thing -- you can't believe how unbelievably thin the beat is. There's nothing to it, no bass! It just goes *bzoot-bzoot-bzoot*. But this tape could make it *hard core*.	“不，是这么回事——你绝对想象不到那个节拍有多弱。什么都没有，连低音都没有！听起来就只像是*动次-动次-动次*。不过这些磁带可以让它变得*硬核*起来。”
0x01000021000000EF	Dialogue Text	PAWNSHOP / ROY	Bird's Nest Roy	Man, you're really invested in this." He looks at the bundle of tape in front of him. It shimmers under the shop's dazzling light show.
Zh: “伙计，你还真是很投入啊。”他看着面前那一盘散开的磁带。它在商店眼花缭乱的灯光秀下闪闪发光。

En: (Look at the clock.) "Wait, this took more than just 'a few moments'. That was at least fifteen minutes!"
Zh: （看看时钟。）“等等，这可不止‘一会儿’吧。至少有15分钟。”

En: Yeah. He nods. "It was. Re-spooling isn't that difficult, although I had to mend the tape in a few places."
Zh: “是啊。”他点点头。“是这样的。把它重新卷回去不难，不过上面有些地方还要修一下。”

En: Anyhow, it's yours now... He slides the tape closer to you on the counter top.
Zh: “不管怎么说，现在它是你的了……”他把磁带滑了过来，推到柜台面上靠近你的地方。

En: Hey, those visuals you've got here would look great in the church. (Point at the lights in the pawnshop.)
Zh: “我说，你这里的视觉效果放在教堂里一定会很棒的。”（指着当铺里的灯光。）

En: I met some young ravers near the place. They want to turn the church into a nightclub and play some weird neo-disco beats there -- they call it *anodic dance music*. I promised to help them with that.
Zh: “我在那附近遇到一群年轻的派对动物。他们想把教堂变成夜店，在那里播放古怪的新式迪斯科音乐——他们把那个叫做*阳极舞曲*。我答应过要帮他们。”

En: I met some young ravers near the place. They want to turn the church into a nightclub and play some weird neo-disco beats there. I promised to help them with that.
Zh: “我在那附近遇到一群年轻的派对动物。他们想把教堂变成夜店，在那里玩些奇怪的新迪斯科音乐。我答应过要帮他们。”

En: Do you know that old church down the coast?
Zh: “你知道海岸边那座教堂吗？”

En: Yes. What about it?
Zh: “是的。怎么了？”

En: I helped some young ravers turn this place into a nightclub. And they play these weird beats there... which they call *anodic music*.
Zh: “我帮一群年轻的派对动物把那地方变成了一家夜店。他们还在那里播放古怪的音乐……他们称之为*阳极舞曲*。”

En: I helped some young ravers turn this place into a nightclub. And they play these weird neo-disco beats there...
Zh: “我要帮一群年轻的派对动物把那个地方变成夜店。在那里玩些奇怪的新迪斯科音乐……”

En: You and me, we could make this all become true. Mankind *needs* this.
Zh: “你和我一起，就能让这一切变成现实。人类*需要*它。”

En: There's a 'but', isn't there? There's always a 'but'.
Zh: 不过总有‘但是’，不是吗？总有一个‘但是’的。

En: But?
Zh: “但是？”

En: Looks like he really means it.
Zh: 他看起来好像是认真的。

En: There's no way to turn this around, I'm afraid.
Zh: 恐怕已经没办法回头了。

En: I have this commemorative pin, but before I sell it I have some questions. (Show him Lena's pin.)
Zh: “我有个纪念别针，不过在卖掉它之前，我有几个问题。”（把莉娜的别针拿给他看。）

En: He studies the pin carefully, rolling it around in his palm. "Saint-Batiste Summer Dinghy Races '31. What a pleasant time *that* was."
Zh: 他仔细研究着别针，在掌心里翻来覆去的查看。“31年圣巴蒂斯特夏季小艇竞赛。*那*可真是个好时代啊。”

En: So, what do you want to know? Price? I can give you 3.20 for it.
Zh: “所以，你想知道什么？价格？我可以给你3.2雷亚尔。”

En: 3.20? Hey, that's a rip-off!
Zh: “3.20？喂，你这是剥削！”

En: A pin from '31 ought to be priced at 3.10 -- but I added another ten cents, just to be nice.
Zh: “31年的别针就应该定价为3.1雷亚尔——不过我又加了10分钱，这样好听些。”

En: Okay, but can you hold onto it for a few days before putting it up for sale? I promised to give the pin back...
Zh: “好吧，但是上架之前能不能先留个几天？我保证过会把别针还回去……”

En: He thinks for a moment, then nods. "Sure, I can keep it behind the counter for a week -- but only if you pawn it right now."
Zh: 他想了一会儿，然后点点头。“当然，我可以把它放在柜台后面，留一星期——不过除非你能现在就当掉它。”

En: That's all I wanted to know about the pin, thanks.
Zh: “别针的事就了解到这里，谢谢。”

En: Sure thing. So, do you want to sell anything?
Zh: “当然了。所以，你想卖点什么吗？”

En: Here you go, officer. He hands you the pin. "Anything else I can do for you today?"
Zh: “给你，警官。”他把别针交给你。“今天还有什么能为你做的吗？”

En: The lieutenant shifts from one foot to another, alert.
Zh: 警督警觉地将一只脚倚在另一只脚上。

En: Miraculously, his face does not reveal what's happening inside.
Zh: 出乎意料的是，他的脸上没有显露出他的内心发生了什么。

En: I'm sorry. I'm sorry I sold my gun.
Zh: “我很抱歉。很抱歉我把自己的枪卖掉了。”

En: He sighs. "Yeah, it's not good. I do hope we manage to clean this mess up somehow -- while also keeping our focus on the murder investigation."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“是啊，这样不好。我真的希望我们能想办法解决这个烂摊子——同时还要持续关注我们的凶杀案调查。”

En: If you say so...
Zh: “如果你这么说的话……”

En: Yes, we'd like to sell these hubcaps, the lieutenant steps in and hands him the spinners.
Zh: “是的，我们想卖掉这些轮圈，”警督上前一步，把东西递给了他。

En: You can sell things from your ITEMS tab for cash -- but be careful: You might wish you had them later.
Zh: 你可以售卖物品栏中的物品以获取现金。但注意：你可能以后会用到它们。

En: Anything else you're thinking of selling? Both the lieutenant and the pawnbroker turn to you.
Zh: “你还有什么想卖的吗？”警督和当铺老板都转向了你。

En: Yes! Fortunately, I still have it. 3.30 and it's yours.
Zh: “是的！很幸运，它还在我这里。3.30雷亚尔，它就是你的了。”

En: 3.30? But I sold it for 3.20...
Yes, you did, but this is how pawnshops make profit... He tilts his head, waiting. "So, do you want it or not?"
Zh: “是的，没错，不过当铺就是这么盈利的……”他歪着头，等待着。“所以，你想不想要了？”

En: You... uhh...
You said you've done it before, yeah?
Zh: “你说自己之前用过的，不是吗？”

En: Have you tried it before?
Zh: “你以前试过它们吗？”

En: Actually, that's all I've got.
Zh: “其实，我只知道这些了。”

En: The pawnbroker's gaze is already fixed on the dancing colours.
Zh: 当铺老板的目光已经固定在跳跃的颜色上了。

En: Someone else came here earlier today asking the same question -- I promptly sold her the gun you pawned a couple days back.
Zh: “今天早些时候，也有人来问过相同的问题——我立马就把你几天前当掉的枪卖给她了。”

En: Have people from Evrart Claire's Union come here to track that gun?
Zh: “艾弗拉特·克莱尔工会的人有没有来找过那把枪？”

En: Maybe. Shady looking guys came in here yesterday, looking like they'd just taken off their Wild Pines overalls. They asked if I have a police weapon to sell. I told them I already sold it.
Zh: “也许吧。昨天来过一个形迹可疑的人，看起来像是刚刚脱掉野松公司的工作服。他们问我这里有没有警察的武器卖。我告诉他们已经卖掉了。”

En: Wait, then it might be true – Evrart's claims.
Zh: 等等，那艾弗拉特的证言——就有可能是真的。

En: Maybe Claire really is tracking down your gun. The lieutenant's eyebrows rise slightly. "Hm..."
Zh: “也许克莱尔真的在追查你的枪。”警督略略抬起了眉头。“嗯……”

En: People as oleaginous as Evrart seem like they're lying even when they're really being truthful.
Zh: 像艾弗拉特一样油嘴滑舌的人，即使真的很真诚，看起来也像是在撒谎一样。

En: They went their way. It was a trip, but you know. All sorts of people come here, asking for all sorts of things.
Zh: “他们有自己的路要走。这是一次旅行，但是你也知道的。各种各样的人来到这里，想要找各种各样的东西。”

En: Thanks for the review. Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “谢谢你帮我们回顾了一下。现在来聊聊别的吧。”

En: Sure, man.
Zh: “当然可以，朋友。”

En: I wanted to ask about my, uh... missing gun. Again.
Zh: “我想问问我的……呃……丢失的枪。再问一次。”

En: Sure thing.
Zh: “当然可以。”

En: Here you go, man. He presents a large cap-shaped object on the palm of his hand. Very odd-looking...
Zh: “给你，伙计。”他伸出手，手掌心上有一个大的帽状物体。看起来很古怪……

En: He's well composed, but underneath it you sense *psychedelic* processes, bubbling. Some kind of drug maybe?
Zh: 他看起来很镇定，但是在那之下，你感觉到*迷幻*的成分在翻腾。也许是某种毒品？

En: Do you know anything about the traffic menace on the loose?
Zh: “你认不认识那个逍遥法外的马路杀手？”

En: His courtesy is not insincere, but he prefers being alone with his projector, just watching the movement of light across the walls of the shop.
Zh: 他的礼貌并非不真诚，他只是更乐意独自一人和投影仪待在一起，就这么看着灯光在店铺的墙面上运动。

En: I have other business to take care of now. [Leave.]
Zh: “我现在还有其他事要做。”[离开。]

En: Is Roy high -- and if yes, then what is he on?
Zh: 罗伊是嗑嗨了吗——如果是，那他嗑了什么？

En: Roy's prices are strange.
Zh: 罗伊的价格很奇怪。

En: Roy doesn't really sleep.
Zh: 罗伊没有真睡着。

En: Odd brain activity.
Zh: 诡异的大脑活动。

En: Composure noticed psychedelics.
Zh: 从容自若注意到了迷幻剂。

En: Strange tie incident.
Zh: 奇怪的领带事件。

En: Dresscode: Insane silk dragon.
Zh: 着装要求：丝绸狂龙。

En: Nope, still no clue. You obviously haven't been doing enough drugs lately.
Zh: 不对，还是没线索。你最近显然嗑的药不够。

En: Feeling warm and enthralled by the movement of light, while the mind continues to race forward? Lucky bastard -- he's probably on Pyrholidon. It's tough to come by on the street.
Zh: 灯光的运动让人感觉温暖而沉醉，然而思想还在继续向前飞奔？这个幸运的混蛋——他很可能是嗑了吡嚯浣酮。在街上很难找到的。

En: Okay, he definitely *is* high. Whatever it is you've probably done it -- and many other things besides. But you can't cut through the jumble of sensations to get to the answer.
Zh: 好吧，他绝对*是*嗑嗨了。不管是什么，你可能已经试过——还有很多其他的种类。但你无法理清这种混乱的感觉，无法找到答案。

En: Is it just me, or is it really warm in here? (Look around.)
Zh: “只是我个人的感觉，还是这里真的很暖和？”（四下查看。）

En: (Get straight to the point.) "So, where does a man get Pyrholidon these days?"
Zh: （直奔主题。）“所以，现在能在什么地方弄到吡嚯浣酮？”

En: There's a note of indignation in his voice. Interesting.
Zh: 他的声音里透露出一丝愤怒的意味。有点意思。

En: No judgement -- just curious. I probably did loads of Pyrholidon before I lost my memory.
Zh: “不是想评判什么——纯粹只是好奇。我失忆前可能也嗑过很多吡嚯浣酮。”

En: I've *had* to take it, you know, since the People's Pile clean-up, he responds carefully. "I was with the Emergency Relief Brigade."
Zh: “我*必须*服用，你也知道的，自从清理人民反应堆之后，”他小心地回应到。“我曾经是紧急救援队的人。”

En: It must have been tough -- radioactive clean-up.
Zh: “一定很艰难吧——放射物清理。”

En: How did you end up running a pawnshop?
Zh: “那你现在怎么开当铺了呢？”

En: I have something else to sell.
Zh: “我还有别的东西要卖。”

En: There's something I'd like to sell.
Zh: “我有点东西要卖。”

En: Sure, let me have a look.
Zh: “当然可以，让我看看。”

En: Let me have a look.
Zh: “让我看一眼。”

En: He takes a step back, studying you. "How would I know?"
Zh: 他后退了一步，仔细打量着你。“你怎么知道的？”

En: He tilts his head. "I try to keep the shop at a comfortable temperature."
Zh: 他把脑袋歪向一旁。“我试着让店铺保持在一个舒适的温度。”

En: Traffic... I'm sorry, officer, but I don't drive.
Zh: “交通……抱歉，警官，但是我不开车的。”

En: It's pretty obvious that you're under the influence, sir.
Zh: “很明显，你还在它的影响之下啊，先生。”

En: Chill out, man -- I'm a chill out cop who just wants some of what you're having. (Wink.)
Zh: “放松点，老兄——我是个很放松的警察，只想弄点你正在用的东西。”（眨眨眼。）

En: He frowns. "Pyrholidon is just something I... you know, since the People's Pile disaster." He coughs, as if to mark his words.
Zh: 他皱皱眉。“吡嚯浣酮只是某种我……你知道的，自从人民反应堆灾难之后。”他咳嗽一声，像是要佐证自己的话。

En: I was with the Emergency Relief Brigade. Had to take it for radiation sickness.
Zh: “我曾经是紧急救援队的一员。必须服用它治疗辐射病。”

En: I lived by the river since I was a small boy. The Esperance... didn't have the heart to let it all go to shit without trying to *do* something, to help out.
Zh: “我还是个小男孩的时候，曾经住在河边。埃斯佩兰斯河……我不忍心看着一切彻底失败而不去试着*做些*什么，帮上一把。”

En: He hesitates. "There's a reason why everyone's tried to forget any of it ever happened, and why no one has tried to repair or replace the Pile."
Zh: 他有些犹豫。“人们想努力忘记发生过的一切是有原因的，还有为什么要维修或者更换反应堆。”

En: Whose fault was it that the generator failed?
Zh: “发电机故障是谁的过错？”

En: No one's, everyone's... He sighs and shakes his head.
Zh: “不是谁的，是所有人的……”他叹了口气，摇摇头。

En: The clean-up happened fifteen years ago. I was young then. Later my second aunt died, left me this shack and the assorted junk in it.
Zh: “清理发生在15年前。那时候我还很年轻。之后我的二姑去世，给我留下这间棚屋，还有里面各种各样的垃圾。”

En: So I came to Martinaise. People told me don't go there, it's a *shit-hole*. I said: people, we just had a *nuclear pile meltdown*. I'm gonna get as far from Faubourg as I can. Still in the same city, but... He shrugs.
Zh: “所以，我来到了马丁内斯。大家告诉我别去，说那里是个*粪坑*。我说：朋友，我们这里刚发生过*核反应堆熔毁*的灾难。我要尽可能地远离市郊。虽说还是在同一座城市吧，但是……”他耸耸肩。

En: Thank you for telling me. (Conclude.)
Zh: “谢谢你告诉我这些。”(总结。)

En: But didn't you hear it when the traffic menace drove over your roof?
Zh: “但是那个马路杀手开过你家屋顶的时候，难道你没听到吗？”

En: Now that I think about it, I do remember hearing a thunderous noise the other night -- some kind of powerful electric vortex hitting the shop and then moving on...
Zh: “这么一想，我确实记起那晚听见了雷鸣一样的噪音——就好像某种强电漩涡击中了店铺，然后继续向前走了……”

En: He shrugs. "I like theory more than story. Outward movement, not vortices."
Zh: 他耸耸肩。“相比故事，我更喜欢理论。向前走，而不是沉入漩涡。”

En: Someone else came here earlier today asking the same question -- I promptly sold her the gun you pawned a couple days back.
Zh: “今天早些时候，也有人来问过相同的问题——我立马就把你几天前当掉的枪卖给她了。”

En: Wait, I *sold* you my gun?
Zh: “等等，我把枪*卖*给你了？”

En: Was the buyer a policeman too?
Zh: “买家也是个警察吗？”

En: Any idea where I can find this buyer?
Zh: “知不知道我能去哪儿找到这个买家？”

En: At least now I know how I lost my sidearm. Let's talk about something else. (Conclude.)
Zh: “至少现在我知道自己的武器是怎么丢的了。我们聊聊别的吧。”(总结。)

En: You were adamant about getting rid of it, officer. He hesitates. "Said you were *undeserving* of a service weapon of the Revachol Citizens Militia."
Zh: “你很坚定地要把它处理掉，警官。”他有些犹豫。“你说自己*不配*拥有瑞瓦肖公民武装的武器。”

En: And I don't like keeping guns around the shop for long. Off-the-charts photon emissions. The unhealthy kind.
Zh: “而我呢，也不想把枪留在店里太久。光子辐射都破纪录了。一点都不健康。”

En: She didn't seem like a policeman, although she kept referring to herself as a *Pig*. Which was odd. I found her interest in the gun a bit... obsessive. But I was just happy to get rid of it. And of her.
Zh: “她看起来不像是警察，不过她一直称呼自己是*猪猪女侠*，这一点很奇怪了。我发现她对枪有点……痴迷，不过我还是很高兴能摆脱它，还有她。”

En: Truth be told, she was terrifying.
Zh: “说句实话，她有点吓人。”

En: Right, so let me get this right, the lieutenant turns to you, "you sold your sidearm, issued by the Citizens Militia, and now a civilian is running around the streets of Martinaise with it."
Zh: “好吧，所以让我来捋一下，”警督转向你，“你卖掉了公民武装配发给你的武器，结果现在有一个市民带着它在马丁内斯的街道上四处游荡。”

En: There is only one explanation. She must be one of my rabid fans.
Zh: “只有一种解释。她肯定是我的狂热粉丝。”

En: Maybe she's a vigilante. Wants to prove she can do our jobs better than we can.
Zh: “也许她是个义务警员。想要证明自己能做的比我们好。”

En: I don't like it either. What if she intends to commit a crime and blame it on the Citizens Militia?
Zh: “我也不喜欢这样。如果她打算犯罪，然后把罪过推到公民武装上面呢？”

En: She must be one of my adoring fans. Just wanted to feel closer to me, you know.
Zh: “她肯定仰慕我的一个粉丝。只是想感觉离我近一点，你懂得。”

En: Maybe she's a vigilante. Wants to prove she can patrol the streets more effectively than the Citizens Militia.
Zh: “也许她是个义务警员，想证明自己在巡逻街道的时候，能比公民武装更有效率。”

En: What if she intends to commit a crime and blame it on the Citizens Militia? On *me*?
Zh: “如果她打算犯罪，然后把罪过推给公民武装，推给*我*呢？”

En: You gotta help me find her, man.
Zh: “你一定要帮我找到她，伙计。”

En: In that case, she'll be happy to get a visit from us. *If* we can find her... He looks around, worried.
Zh: “如果那样的话，她一定很乐意我们的到访。*如果*我们能找到她的话……”他四下看了看，有些担心。

En: It's possible in these parts of town. We ought to find her and discourage her from taking justice into her own hands. He looks around, worried.
Zh: “她可能就在城里。我们必须找到她，阻止她亲手主持正义。”他四下看了看，有些担心。

En: You're right that she could cast aspersions on the force. We have to find her. He looks around, worried.
Zh: “你说得对，她有可能会诽谤警察。我们必须找到她。”他四下看了看，有些担心。

En: Sure, man.
Zh: “当然了，朋友。”

En: Oh... I certainly hope not.
Zh: “哦……我肯定希望不要这样。”

En: He tenses. "I hadn't considered that... This is why I don't like to deal with guns."
Zh: 他绷紧了神经。“我没有考虑到这个……这就是我真心不喜欢处理枪的原因。”

En: I wish I could help with that, officer, but I don't know where she was from or where she was going.
Zh: “我希望能帮到你，警官，但是我不知道她从哪里来，也不知道她要到哪里去。”

En: He considered it, but his priority was to get rid of the gun as quickly as possible -- albeit for a price.
Zh: 他考虑过，但他的优先级是尽快处理掉那把枪——不过钱还是要的。

En: He's trying to ingratiate himself with you by anticipating your questions. He knows he shouldn't have sold the gun.
Zh: 他想通过预期你的问题来讨好你。他知道自己不应该卖掉那支枪的。

En: He's doubtful, but doesn't see the point in contradicting a notion that is so dear to you.
Zh: 他有些怀疑，但是觉得没必要反驳对你来说很珍贵的概念。

En: He steps further back behind the bullet-resistant glass, visibly perturbed. He doesn't want to be involved in any *incidents*.
Zh: 他在防弹玻璃后面又后退了一大步，明显有些焦虑。他不想不卷入任何*事件*里。

En: My apologies, officer, but I have no idea where she was coming from or where she went.
Zh: “我很抱歉，警官，但是我不知道她从哪里来，也不知道她要到哪里去。”

En: So much disappointment -- and early deaths, cancer mostly. And we knew all that was coming even as we were *cleaning up* as best we could.
Zh: “有太多事情令人沮丧了——许多人英年早逝，大多死于癌症。我们已经尽最大努力*清理*，却也知道这一切终将来临。”

En: The People's Pile? What's that?
Zh: “人民反应堆？那是什么？”

En: Tell me more about this Emergency Relief Brigade you were part of.
Zh: “跟我说说你参加的那个紧急救援队。”

En: He points at the white triangle on his orange safety jacket. "We were an all-volunteer force, self-organized. Tried to help fire brigades contain the spill."
Zh: 他指着橙色反光夹克上的白三角。“我们是一支完全由志愿者自发组成的队伍。想要帮助消防队控制泄露。”

En: That sure narrows down our list of suspects.
Zh: “这样确实就缩小了我们的嫌疑人的名单。”

En: Does that mean you don't have any idea who the driver was?
Zh: “这是不是意味着你也不知道司机是谁？”

En: He shakes his head slowly.
Zh: 他慢慢摇摇头。

En: A vortex? Sounds fascinating.
Zh: “漩涡？听起来很迷人啊。”

En: Yeah, it was pretty wild. I didn't really know what to make of it, but I know it meant *something*.
Zh: “是啊，真的挺疯狂的。我真的不知道是什么发出来的，但我知道这意味着*什么*。”

En: Wait, why don't you drive?
Zh: “等等，你为什么不开车？”

En: I just don't like it very much. Movement on the road never really gelled well with the movement of my thoughts.
Zh: “我就是不太喜欢而已。公路上的活动从来没能跟我的思维运行很好地结合在一起。”

En: Yet another sign that the end is nigh... It must be destiny that brought me here. Tell me more about this vortex?
Zh: “又是世界末日即将到来的迹象……肯定是命运把我带到了这里。再跟我多说说这个漩涡的事？”

En: The end... I don't know about that. Seemed more like a sign of a large-scale transformation. Shook the entire shack.
Zh: “世界末日……那我可不知道。更像是大范围改革的讯号。整个小屋都跟着在摇晃。”

En: I don't have anything to sell at the moment.
Zh: “我现在没什么要卖的。”

En: Anything else you're thinking of selling?
Zh: “你还有什么想卖的吗？”

En: I'd like to sell my clothes.
Zh: “我要卖掉我的衣服。”

En: I have a fascinating photo of a corpse here. (Show him the photo.)
Zh: “我这里有一张迷人的尸体相片。”（把相片拿给他看。）

En: I'm not purchasing any more clothing at the moment... He looks you up and down quickly.
Zh: “我目前不打算再多买衣服了……”他快速地上下打量了你一番。

En: Suddenly, his gaze fixes on your tie. He steps back from the glass. "And *especially* that tie. It swallows photons around it. I have no need for *necrotic* objects."
Zh: 突然，他的目光固定在你的领带上。玻璃后的他后退了一步。“*特别*是那条领带。它会吞噬周围所有的光子。我不需要这种*坏死*的东西。”

En: You might be able to aid our investigation.
Zh: “也许你能协助我们的调查。”

En: You might be able to aid my investigation.
Zh: “也许你能协助我的调查。”

En: I doubt it, but I can try to answer any questions you may have.
Zh: “我对此表示怀疑，但是我可以尽力去回答你的任何问题。”

En: Know anything about the recent hanging?
Zh: “知道最近那个吊起来的男人吗？”

En: Think you could help me get a corpse out of a tree?
Zh: “你能不能帮我把尸体从树上搬下来？”

En: Oh... no, I don't like those kinds of objects. No sale. He barely even looks at it.
Zh: “哦……不，我不喜欢那些东西。不能卖的。”他甚至连看都没看一眼。

En: You've surprised me more than once during the course of our investigation, but I have to say it still seems like a *remote* scenario. He shrugs.
Zh: 警督耸耸肩。“我们调查的时候你已经让我震惊过很多回了，但我还是想说，这事儿*实在*太遥远了……”

En: To be perfectly frank, I have my reservations about this whole antenna scheme. But who knows? We may yet be surprised.
Zh: “说实话，我对天线的计划持保留意见。但谁说得清？没准会有惊喜呢……”

En: I'm more interested in *why* you're so fixated on this idea in the first place.
Zh: “我更感兴趣的是，*为什么*你会对这事这么执着。”

En: But here's the thing, detective. *We're* the proper channels. Doing our work, right here on the ground, is how we'll solve this case. There's no need to bring the Coalition into this.
Zh: “但事情是这样的，警探。我们的*名正言顺*的，解决这个案子的办法就是光明正大地做自己的工作。没必要把联盟给扯进来。”

En: Let me make a prediction, detective. You are not going to see the inside of a Coalition warship anytime soon.
Zh: “我来给你猜猜吧，警探。你一时半会是见不到联盟战舰里头什么样的。”

En: We can't assign *la responsabilité* if we don't go through the proper channels.
Zh: “要是我们名不正言不顺，那就没法分配*职责*了。”

En: To be honest, I mostly just want to ride on Coalition Warship Archer.
Zh: “说实话，我只想搭上联盟国战舰弓箭手号。”

En: I certainly understand the impulse, but this is our job, detective. Our whole *raison d'être*. At some point, we'll need to return to it.
Zh: “我能理解这种冲动，但这是我们的工作，警探。是我们*存在的理由*。我们迟早都得回到这上面来的。”

En: But now you've got what you came for, so I suppose we'll have to see this business through.
Zh: “但既然你已经拿到了想要的东西，或许我们能把这事儿办成吧。”

En: I think I just need a break from our main case for a bit.
Zh: “我可能得先把手头的主要案子放一放了。”

En: The pawnbroker turns from you to the lieutenant and back again, a distant look on his face.
Zh: 当铺老板一脸冷漠地看了看警督，又看了看你。

En: By the way -- do you happen to have any guns, like the ones carried by officers of the Citizens Militia?
Zh: “话说……你这里不会刚好有卖枪吧，比如公民武装警察带的那种？”

En: By the way... do you, um, happen to have any *guns*? Like the ones carried by cops?
Zh: “话说……你这里，呃，不会碰巧有卖*枪*吧？比如警察带的那种？”

En: He's not telling you the whole story. It seems he's trying to spare you.
Zh: 他没有把全部的故事告诉你，就像他想要放过你一样。

En: I feel like there's something you're not telling me.
Zh: “我觉得你还有一些事没告诉我。”

En: He probably has a good reason for that. (Drop the matter.)
Zh: 也许他有很好的理由吧。（不再谈这件事。）

En: He looks away. "You weren't quite yourself, officer."
Zh: 他看向远处。“你不太对劲，警官。”

En: You sucked on a gun? Good. Good. Very normal.
Zh: 你舔枪？好。很好。很常见嘛。

En: What was I like?
Zh: “我什么样？”

En: Happens to the best of us, I guess.
Zh: “我猜，最优秀的人身上确实会发生这种事。”

En: I'm so sorry you had to see me in that state. We don't have to talk about it any further.
Zh: “抱歉让你看到我那个样子。我们不用再深入讨论了。”

En: No apologies necessary, officer.
Zh: “没必要道歉，警官。”

En: It certainly does, especially in Revachol.
Zh: “的确是，特别是在瑞瓦肖。”

En: You were very distraught. You said the gun was a threat to your life, and... that you can't *trust* yourself with it tonight. And you need the money.
Zh: “你真的很心烦意乱。你说枪对于你的生活是威胁，还有……今晚你不*相信*带着它的自己。而且你需要钱。”

En: When I said that I don't normally buy firearms, you put the gun barrel in your mouth and sort of... sucked on it. Then I agreed to take it.
Zh: “当我说我一般不买武器的时候，你把枪管塞进了嘴里，好像是……在舔它。然后我就答应收下了。”

En: Wait... how *much* did I sell the gun for?
Zh: “等等……我把枪卖了*多少钱*？”

En: Wait... how much did I sell the gun for?
Zh: “等等……我把枪卖了多少钱？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: The light swirls in his face and glasses. He doesn't know what to say either.
Zh: 灯光在他的脸庞和眼镜上旋转。他也不知道该说什么好。

En: Fifteen reál.
The lieutenant looks from you to Roy and then back to you. It's clear that he hopes this tableau might still turn out to be a bad dream -- it's not, though.
Zh: 警督看向罗伊，然后又回望向你。显然他希望这个戏剧性的场面也许只是一场噩梦——不过，它不是。

En: What is a particle decay generator?
Zh: 粒子衰变发电机是什么？

En: Why is it called the *People's* Pile?
Zh: 为什么叫*人民*反应堆？

En: Why did it fail? (Conclude.)
Zh: 为什么会失败？（总结。）

En: A primitive nuclear reactor, also called a *pile*.
Zh: 一个原始的核反应堆，也被称为*反应堆*。

En: Construction began during the Commune of Revachol. The *people* continued work on it after the Commune fell -- they wanted a cheap source of energy for Revachol West.
Zh: 建造是在瑞瓦肖公社时期开始的。公社覆灭之后，*人们*还在继续工作——他们希望瑞瓦肖西部能够拥有廉价的能源。

En: An emergency valve defect resulted in steam pressure blowing the turbine, taking the fuel containment vessel up in the explosion. Both the faulty design and lack of finances contributed to the catastrophe.
Zh: 一个有缺陷的安全阀导致涡轮机内的蒸汽压力飙升，炸开了燃料安全壳。设计缺陷和资金缺乏共同引发了那场灾难。

En: With Kim here too?! That just sounded really, really bad.
Zh: 金还在这里啊？！这个听起来真的，真的不是什么好主意。

En: Do you know what the tattoos mean?
Zh: “你知道这个纹身的意思吗？”

En: He frowns. "Aphotic paths? Counter-radiance networks? Antimagnetism? It's darkness. That's all I know. Sell me something *lighter*."
Zh: 他皱紧眉头。“无光路径？反辐射网络？反磁性？它是黑暗的，我只知道这一点。卖点*更光明*的东西给我吧。”

En: Another time, perhaps.
Zh: “也许改天吧。”

En: I didn't mean to offend you. (Drop the subject -- you don't want drugs.)
Zh: “我不想冒犯你。”（放弃这个话题——你不想要毒品。）

En: His loosens up. "I'm not offended, officer."
Zh: 他的姿势松懈了很多。“我没有被冒犯。警官。”

En: Let's talk about you sharing your Pyrholidon again.
Zh: “我们再聊聊分享吡嚯浣酮的事吧。”

En: Care to share your Pyrholidon with me?
Zh: “能不能分我一点吡嚯浣酮？”

En: It's almost like he's worried for you.
Zh: 他甚至好像有点担心你。

En: I ride the *pyrho* every day.
Zh: “我每天都用*吡咯*。”

En: Every day? He tilts his head, studying you, then nods. "You look the part."
Zh: “每天？”他歪着头打量你，然后点点头。“你看上去像是那种人。”

En: Here you go, man. He presents a large cap-shaped object on the palm of his hand. Very odd-looking...
Zh: “给你，伙计。”他伸出手，手掌心上有一个大的盖状物体。看起来很古怪……

En: Yes, darling, that's the Coalition Government ordained Pyrholidon -- straight into your gut!
Zh: 没错，亲爱的，那就是联盟政府规定的吡嚯浣酮——能直接进入你的内脏！

En: Not so sure about this.
Zh: 我不太确定。

En: Wait, why wouldn't I be sure?
Zh: 等等，为什么我不确定？

En: Because it's an anti-radiation drug and you're a cop, not a post-apocalyptic scavenger?
Zh: 因为这是一种抗辐射药物，而你是个警察，不是后启示录时代的清道夫？

En: I'll take it this time. (Take the pyrholidon.)
Zh: “这次我决定收下。”（收下吡嚯浣酮。）

En: Thanks, man. (Take the pyrholidon.)
Zh: “谢了，伙计。”（拿走吡嚯浣酮。）

En: Actually, that's still a *no on the pyrholidon for me." (Refuse to take the pyrholidon for now.)
Zh: “其实吧，我还是要对吡嚯浣酮说*不*。”（暂时拒绝收下吡嚯浣酮。）

En: Actually, I changed my mind. I don't need any Pyrholidon. (Refuse to take the pyrholidon for now.)
Zh: “其实，我改主意了。我不需要什么吡嚯浣酮。”（暂时拒绝吡嚯浣酮。）

En: He looks down. "Of course."
Zh: 他低下头。“没事。”

En: I respect your decision, officer. What else can I help you with?
Zh: “我尊重你的决定，警官。还有什么我能帮你的吗？”

En: I can understand how you would mistake Jacob Irw for a powerful electric vortex.
Zh: “我可以理解，你把雅各布·欧文错当成强电漩涡了。”

En: Jacob Irw? The great TipTop champion? Yes, yes...
Zh: “雅各布·欧文？那个伟大的大师赛冠军？是的，没错……”

En: His forehead creases. "Do you think it may have been... Jacob Irw who drove over your roof?"
Zh: 他皱起了前额。“你觉得会不会是……雅各布·欧文开车经过你的屋顶？”

En: It's a logical explanation. I felt within that thunderous crash a soul plunged into chaos.
Zh: “这是个很合逻辑的解释。我感觉在那次电闪雷鸣般的撞击中，一个灵魂陷入了混沌之中。”

En: I felt within that thunderous crash a soul plunged into chaos.
Zh: “我感觉在那次电闪雷鸣般的撞击中，一个灵魂陷入了混沌之中。”

En: The vortex is going to become much bigger -- it is an integral part of how it all ends.
Zh: “漩涡会变得越来越大——它是一切走向灭亡时不可或缺的一部分。”

En: An *end* implies a stable state, whereas we can see from experience that everything is in fact always transforming. Thus, there can be no end.
Zh: “*终结*意味着一个稳定状态，然而从过去的经验可以看出，一切总是在不停变化的。因此，就不会有所谓的终结。”

En: But that is merely our meagre sense experience. There are dark forces at work now that we cannot even envision, much less see.
Zh: “但那不过是我们那贫乏的感觉体验。现在黑暗势力正在暗潮汹涌，我们甚至无法想象，就更谈不上亲眼所见了。”

En: The dark always has its counterbalance in light. Neither will prevail. The cycle will continue. He folds his arms with finality and turns away from you, concentrating once more on his light show.
Zh: “黑暗总能在光明中找到平衡，但永远无法获胜。循环将会继续。”他抱起双臂，然后转身离开，又一次专注他的灯光秀去了。

En: The corpse behind the hostel, I assume... He looks into the swirling lights, then to you: "I don't have a truck with a mounted platform or anything of that sort myself..."
Zh: “旅社背后的尸体，我猜……”他看着旋转的灯光，然后望向你：“我自己没有带安装平台的货車，或者类似的东西……”

En: Ask around the harbour. There might be some workers there who'd be willing to help.
Zh: “去港口问问吧。也许有些工人愿意帮忙。”

En: I do my best to keep my distance from all manner of butchery. Bad for business, bad for everyone.
Zh: “我尽力与各种各样的杀戮保持距离。对生意不好，对所有人都不好。”

En: 'This mess' -- he means *your* mess.
Zh: 这个烂摊子——他的意思是*你的*烂摊子。

En: Needless to say: things didn't work out.
Zh: 不用说：事情没有成功。

En: Roy takes the hubcaps from the lieutenant and inspects them. The spinners appear iridescent in the dancing light of his pawnshop.
Zh: 罗伊从警督手里接过轮圈，仔细端详起来。轮圈在当铺跳跃的灯光下，显得格外的色彩斑斓。

En: He marvels at the cobalt shimmer, and nods: "Yes, these are very, very good. Did you defraud some foreign prince for them? Jump a Mesque banger? No matter... I'll give you 200 reál."
Zh: 他对钴的光芒感到惊奇，然后点点头：“是的，这些真的特别，特别好。你这是从哪个外国王子那里诈骗来的吗？还是打劫了一个梅斯克暴徒？无所谓了……我可以付给你200雷亚尔。”

En: Thank you. He takes the cash, then turns to you.
Zh: “谢谢。”他接过现金，然后转向了你。

En: Here's the 30 reál you need for your bill. Do *not* waste it. The rest goes in his pocket.
Zh: “这是30雷亚尔，给你还账单的。*不要*浪费了。”他把剩下的钱塞进自己的口袋。

En: Here's the 60 reál you need for your bill. Do *not* waste it. The rest goes in his pocket.
Zh: “这是60雷亚尔，给你还账单的。*不要*浪费了。”他把剩下的钱塞进自己的口袋。

En: Here's the 70 reál you need for your bill. Do *not* waste it. The rest goes in his pocket.
Zh: “这是70雷亚尔，给你还账单的。*不要*浪费了。”他把剩下的钱塞进自己的口袋。

En: Here's the 100 reál you need for your bill. Do *not* waste it. The rest goes in his pocket.
Zh: “这是100雷亚尔，给你还账单的。*不要*浪费了。”他把剩下的钱塞进自己的口袋。

En: Here's the 130 reál you need for your bill. Do *not* waste it. The rest goes in his pocket.
Zh: “这是130雷亚尔，给你还账单的。*不要*浪费了。”他把剩下的钱塞进自己的口袋。

En: No one was defrauded or *jumped*, I assure you, the lieutenant explains as the pawnbroker opens the register and counts the cash.
Zh: “没有人被诈骗，或者是*被打劫*，我可以向你保证，”警督解释到。当铺老板打开收银机，开始数现金。

En: Of course. I meant no offense. 200 reál for you, officer. Delightful doing business with you. Do come again.
Zh: “当然。我无意冒犯。给你200雷亚尔，警官。能跟你做生意，我很高兴。记得下次再来啊。”

En: Hello again. How can I help you?
Zh: “你好啊。有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: He looks off to the side, then down at his hands. "Can't say I'm a huge admirer of Wild Pines, and I certainly wouldn't trust any silver-tongued spokesperson of theirs. Fortunately, I have no reason to get involved."
Zh: 他看向一边，然后低头看着自己的手。“不能说我是野松公司的铁杆粉丝，而且我确实也不相信那些舌灿莲花的发言人。幸运的是，我也没有介入其中的理由。”

En: Ever had any dealings with Evrart Claire?
Zh: “你跟艾弗拉特・克莱尔做过什么交易吗？”

En: Ever had any dealings with the Union Boss?
Zh: “你跟工会老大做过什么交易吗？”

En: Know anything about the Wild Pines representative that's in town?
Zh: “你了解野松公司派来这里的代表吗？”

En: He's been by the shop a couple of times. He gives a lazy half-shrug.
Zh: “他路过店铺几次。”他懒懒地耸了耸肩。

En: There was something awfully deliberate about that laziness.
Zh: 这种慵懒完全是经过深思熟虑的。

En: Did he discuss union business with you?
Zh: “他有没有跟你谈过工会的生意？”

En: Did he buy anything?
Zh: “他买过什么吗？”

En: Why do I feel like you don't want to talk about him? Are you hiding something?
Zh: “为什么我感觉你不想提他？你在隐瞒什么吗？”

En: Wait... what do you have against Wild Pines?
Zh: “等等……你对野松公司有什么不满吗？”

En: Have you *met* the Wild Pines rep?
Zh: “你有没有*见过*野松公司的代表？”

En: He tried. Wanted to come to some *mutually beneficial arrangement* around my dealings with the dockworkers... I politely declined to *hear him out*.
Zh: “他试过。想让我跟码头工人达成什么*互惠互利的条件*……我礼貌地拒绝了他，没让他*把话说完*。”

En: He purchased a remarkably garish paperweight the first time he was here... Nothing the second.
Zh: “他第一次来的时候，买了一个特别花哨的镇纸……第二次什么也没买。”

En: They weren't the most... pleasant interactions. Small-town Big Wigs always want everyone to play a part in the play they're staging.
Zh: “算不上是什么……愉快的交流。小城市的大人物总是希望每个人都能参与他们的戏码。”

En: But I bowed out at some point. I prefer to watch from afar while the Big Wigs come and go.
Zh: “但是我中途退出了。我更喜欢站在远处，看着那些大人物们来来去去。”

En: I have other questions about this.
Zh: “关于这个，我还有其他问题。”

En: He nods reluctantly.
Zh: 他不情愿地点点头。

En: So you don't know what kind of arrangement he was talking about? The lieutenant studies the pawnbroker suspiciously.
Zh: “所以你不知道他说的是什么样的条件了？”警督怀疑地打量着当铺老板。

En: As I said, I refused to talk to him. That's why he came back a second time. That's *also* why he hasn't come back a third.
Zh: “我说过了，我拒绝和他交谈。所以他后来又来过一次。这*也*是他没来第三次的原因。”

En: For a moment, his jaw tenses up, and his gaze is sharp and clear. Then he relaxes again.
Zh: 片刻之间，他的下颚紧绷，目光锐利又清晰。随后又再次放松了。

En: What kind of *arrangement*?
Zh: “什么*条件*？”

En: No, just -- people like that make everyone else see the world somewhat askew... at an angle that is convenient for them. Now -- you have more questions, or are we...
Zh: “不，只是——那种人总是想让其他人错误地看待这个世界……选择一个对他们有利的角度。现在——你还有什么问题吗，或者我们就此……”

En: What should I have *for* it?
Zh: “我该拿什么来*换*它呢？”

En: The People's Pile was a Type U particle decay generator that failed immediately after entering service, releasing radioactive waste into River Esperance.
Zh: 人民反应堆是一台U型粒子衰变发电机，一投入使用立马遇到故障，把放射性废物都排进了埃斯佩兰斯河。

En: Those triangle patches on his vest... you have a feeling they *mean* something. Like they're similar to the halogen rectangle on your jacket.
Zh: 他衣服上的那些三角补丁——你感觉它们*意味*着什么。跟你夹克上的卤素灯矩形有点类似。

En: (Point at his clothes.) "Say, what's with the triangles on your vest?"
Zh: （指向他的衣服。）“话说，你衣服上的三角是什么？”

En: He hesitates. "I was... I was with the Emergency Relief Brigade. You know, after the People's Pile disaster." He coughs, as if to mark his words.
Zh: 他有些犹豫。“为何人们努力想忘记发生的一切，以及为何没有人设法去维修或更换反应堆。一切都是有原因的。”他咳嗽一声，像是要记住自己的话。

En: Had to take Pyrholidon for radiation sickness. That's what you were hinting at just now, wasn't it?
Zh: “必须服用吡嚯浣酮来治疗辐射病。你刚才暗示的就是这个，不是吗？”

En: He's taking it for mental and emotional, not physical pain these days.
Zh: 他如今服用这些药物已是为了满足精神和情感需求，而非缓解肉体的疼痛。

En: I'd like to buy back the commemorative pin I sold.
Zh: “我想赎回之前卖掉的纪念别针。”

En: Buy the pin.
Zh: 买回别针。

En: His eyes move from you to the lights dancing on the walls.
Zh: 他的目光从你身上移开，转到墙上那些跳动的光线上。

En: Hey, do you know how to fix this? (Show him the bundle of magnetic tape.)
Zh: “嗨，你知不知道怎么修这个？”（把磁带给他看。）

En: He looks at the bronze-coloured bundle in your hand. "You mean re-spool it? Yeah, I do, but..."
Zh: 他看着你手中那一团青铜色的东西。“你是说把它重新绕好吗？是啊，我会，但是……”

En: He slowly finishes his thought: "...but I'm not some Mr. Fixit, I'm a pawnbroker. If you want to pawn the tape, sure. Although it looks pretty... worthless."
Zh: 他慢慢回过神来：“……但我不是什么能工巧匠，我是个当铺老板。如果你想典当磁带，当然可以。虽然它看起来真的挺……不值钱的。”

En: Hmh, maybe you can help me some other way, then.
Zh: “嗯，那也许你可以用别的方法帮我。”

En: Just explain why you need this so much. He's bound to understand.
Zh: 解释一下你为什么这么需要它。他肯定能理解的。

En: Wait, but you tinker with film tapes all the time. Isn't that the same?
Zh: “等等，但你一直在修补电影胶卷啊。那不是一样吗？”

En: No, it's different... those film tapes actually *mean* something to me, but this is just a worthless bundle of old tape.
Zh: “不，不一样的……其实那些电影胶卷对我来说是有*意义*的，但这不过是一堆不值钱的破磁带。”

En: Is it any good? The music, I mean.
Zh: “有什么好的吗？我的意思是说，那个音乐。”

En: I helped them up the bass beat. It's getting pretty *hard core*, but there's something missing. A melody line to complement the beat. I'm feeling this tape might be it.
Zh: “我帮他们完成了低音节拍，结果变得还挺*硬核*的，不过还是缺了点什么，缺了一条补足节拍的旋律线。我感觉也许这盘磁带就是正解。”

En: Okay, I'll help you out. It's going to take a moment though, so just sit back and relax...
Zh: “好吧，我可以帮你。不过可能要点工夫，你们要不先坐下休息一会儿吧……”

En: Sure... he mumbles as his eyes once again start following the light show around the pawnshop.
Zh: “当然……”他嘟囔了几句，目光又开始追逐当铺里的灯光秀。

En: Yeah, I bet they would... all those lights in a massive church hall... He stares at the intricate interplay of light and shadow all around him, suddenly moved by the idea.
Zh: “是啊，我肯定他们会的……一座巨大教堂大厅里的灯光……”他凝望着周身错综复杂的光影，突然被这个想法打动了。

En: You take some time to look around the store... The play of visuals all around the pawnshop is mesmerizing. Suddenly, Roy turns back to you with a reel of tape in his hand and coughs.
Zh: 你花了点时间四下查看了整个店铺……当铺周围的视觉效果让人目眩神迷。突然间，罗伊转向你，咳嗽了一声，手里还躺着一盘磁带。

En: You know, these visuals would look *super cool* in the church.
Zh: 你知道吗，如果在教堂的话，这些视觉效果一定会*非常酷炫*的。

En: Well, thanks for the help. (Proceed; take the repaired tape.)
Zh: “感谢你的帮助。”（继续；拿走修好的磁带。）

En: Yeah. My pleasure. I do what I can for true passion projects... Just try not to use this tape for negative photon emissions. Take responsibility, okay.
Zh: “乐意效劳。只要是我真正热爱的东西，我都会竭尽全力……只是尽量不要把磁带用在阴性光子发射上。负责一点，好吧。”

En: A sanctuary filled with hand-picked positive photons... There would be no room for sadness in such a place. It's a brilliant vision...
Zh: “一座满是精选阳性光子的圣殿……那种地方肯定容不下任何悲伤。那将是一幅灿烂的景象。”

En: I rather like it *here* too, so yeah -- I'm not giving you my projector. This tape is all I can do for you and your friends' nightclub project. He nods at the fixed tape on the counter top.
Zh: “我也喜欢*这里*，所以——我不会把投影仪给你的。这盘磁带是我能为你和你朋友的夜店计划所做的一切。”他朝着柜台上面那盘修好的磁带点了点头。

En: Yeah, man, no. He shakes his head. "I rather like this place happy too. You know -- where I work. This tape is all I can do for you and your friends' nightclub project." He nods at the fixed tape on the counter top.
Zh: “呃，老兄，不行，”他摇摇头，“我也喜欢这个快乐的地方。你知道的——在我工作的地方。这盘磁带是我能为你和你朋友的夜店计划所做的一切。”他朝着柜台上面那盘修好的磁带点了点头。

En: Poor apes on a stupid ball, with never enough money to do anything remarkable.
Zh: 可怜的猩猩，身在一颗愚蠢的星球，永远也没有足够的钱去做一些非凡的事。

En: I'll check my pockets. (Access your pawn menu.)
Zh: “我看看自己的口袋。”（进入典当菜单。）

En: Between you and me, he looks you up and down, "this is why I don't get involved in local politics."
Zh: “这话就你我之间说说，”他抬头看看你，然后低下头，“这就是我为什么不掺合当地政治的原因。”

En: You're calling this *politics*?
Zh: “你把这个叫*政治*吗？”

En: So it goes. The pawnbroker nods gravely, then shrugs. "Now, what can I do for you?" he asks.
Zh: “就是这么回事。”当铺老板严肃地点点头，然后耸耸肩。“现在，有什么能帮你的吗？”他问到。

En: What can I do for you? he asks.
Zh: “有什么能帮你的吗？”他问道。

En: Who are your customers usually?
Zh: “一般谁是你这里的常客？”

En: It keeps me entertained, he responds, unimpressed with your critique.
Zh: “这样我会比较开心，”他回应道，对你的批评无动于衷。

En: It keeps me entertained, he responds, unimpressed with your business-school snobbery.
Zh: “这样我会比较开心，”他回应道，对你那副故作内行的派头无动于衷。

En: It keeps me entertained, he responds, unimpressed with your snobbery.
Zh: “这样我会比较开心，”他回应道，对你那副故作内行的派头无动于衷。

En: Greetings on this fine night. What brings you here?
Zh: “你好，真是个美好的夜晚。是什么风把你吹来了？”

En: The pawnshop is always open.
Zh: “当铺的门永远是开着的。”

En: You do know that it's not really a 'fine night'? The weather is terrible.
Zh: “你应该知道这算不上是什么‘美好’的夜晚吧？天气实在是太糟糕了。”

En: How come you're still open?
Zh: “你为什么还开着门？”

En: It's cool that you're still open. Big city vibes.
Zh: “你这里还开着门真是太赞了。有大城市的氛围。”

En: Top of the morning to you. How may I be of service?
Zh: “早上好。有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Isn't being forced to get up for work at dawn a little inhumane?
Zh: “强迫自己一大清早就爬起来工作，是不是有点不太人道？”

En: Early bird gets the worm, huh?
Zh: “早起的鸟儿有虫吃，是吗？”

En: I'm so glad you're already awake. It's so lonely in this city at this hour!
Zh: “真高兴你已经醒了。这个点的城市实在是太寂寞了！”

En: Is it? He turns his head to the right and then to the left, as if looking for non-existent windows. "Spring is capricious, yes."
Zh: “是吗？”他把头转向右边，然后是左边，像是在寻找一扇不存在的窗户。“没错，春季就是这么的反复无常。”

En: Perhaps. He shrugs. "I just like being awake for the transition between night and morning -- bearing witness to the first hints of light on the horizon."
Zh: “也许吧。”他耸耸肩。“我只是喜欢在夜晚和早晨过渡的时候保持清醒——亲眼目睹地平线上的第一缕光线。”

En: It's not often that I see officers from the RCM in my pawnshop. The man at the counter turns to you slowly.
Zh: “RCM的警官在我的当铺可不常见。”柜台后的男人慢慢转向你。

En: Oh, no, not at all. He flashes a smile. "I guess I haven't had many customers lately, RCM or otherwise."
Zh: “哦，不，一点也不。”他的脸上闪过一丝微笑。“我猜大概是最近客人比较少，RCM或者其他的都没有。”

En: Who are your customers usually?
Zh: “一般谁是你这里的常客？”

En: Sorry, I feel like I'm interrupting you.
Zh: “抱歉，我好像打扰到你了。”

En: Now that the RCM is here -- tell me, have you had any trouble lately?
Zh: “既然RCM已经来了——告诉我，你最近遇到什么麻烦了吗？”

En: I haven't had any problems myself... though some of my customers have complained about inconsistent law enforcement.
Zh: “我自己真的没什么问题……不过有些客人在抱怨什么执法矛盾。”

En: All kinds of people come through here... Locals, travellers. People looking for a deal. People looking for a keepsake. People who are terminally bored.
Zh: “各种各样的人都会来我这里……本地人，游客。想做交易的人。想找纪念品的人。无聊至极的人。”

En: I see you were wounded in the recent... altercation. He nods gravely at your leg. "A speedy recovery to you, officer."
Zh: “我看你在最近的……争执事件里受伤了。”他看着你的腿，严肃地点点头。“希望你能早日康复，警官。”

En: Now, what can I do for you?
Zh: “现在，我有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: As you can see... I have a wide selection of goods for everyone to choose from.
Zh: “你也看见了……我这里的商品种类比较多，能让每个人慢慢挑选。”

En: (Shake your head.) "It's shameful, how insufficient the police presence is in these parts!"
Zh: （摇摇头。）“有点惭愧，这些地区的警力还真是不够充足啊！”

En: This business plan is all over the place. You should specialize, zoom in, recapitalize.
Zh: “这个商业计划太分散了。你应该细化，聚焦，重新调整结构。”

En: I see you were wounded in the recent... altercation, he says with a grave nod. "A speedy recovery to you, officer."
Zh: “我看你在最近的……争执事件里受伤了。”他看着你的腿，严肃地点点头。“希望你能早日康复，警官。”

En: Hello hello! Let me know if I can help you with anything.
Zh: “好啊好啊！如果你需要什么帮助的话，尽管开口。”

En: It keeps me entertained. His attention is drawn once more to the play of light and shadow on the walls behind you.
Zh: “这让我很开心。”他的注意力再一次被你身后墙面上的光影吸引走了。

En: (Look around.) "Typical vacuous consumerist objects."
Zh: （环顾四周。）“典型的空虚消费主义物品。”

En: Honestly, I think some of your selections are... (Press your finger to your lips) "...more tasteful than others."
Zh: “说实话，我觉得你这里有些选择……”（把手指压在嘴唇上）“……比其他的更有品味一些。”

En: Quite the collection. I *may* have something to add to it...
Zh: “的确是很壮观的收藏。我*也许*可以帮你加一点进去……”

En: (Nod.) "Quite the collection indeed."
Zh: （点头。）“的确是很壮观的收藏。”

En: Well, someone has to.
Zh: “呃，总得有人这样吧。”

En: Yeah, I shouldn't have either.
Zh: “是啊，我也不应该。”

En: In its purest form. Now, what can I do for you?
Zh: “最纯粹的形式。现在，有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: I really should have killed myself. Maybe I still will one of these days.
Zh: “我真的应该杀了我自己。也许某天我会这样做的。”

En: The light swirls in his face and glasses. He doesn't know what to say.
Zh: 灯光在他的脸庞和眼镜上旋转。他也不知道该说什么好。

En: Are you sure you don't want to buy some armour?
Zh: “你确定不想买些盔甲吗？”

En: I have some armour...
Zh: “我有些盔甲……”

En: He knits his eyebrows, thinking, then says, "Alright, let's see it."
Zh: 他皱起眉头，想了想，然后说到，“好吧，让我看看。”

En: I've got these sabatons. (Show him.)
Zh: “我这儿有护脚甲。”（给他看。）

En: Just look at this awesome cuirass. (Show him.)
Zh: “看看这胸甲，多棒啊。”（给他看。）

En: I've got a helmet. (Show him.)
Zh: “我有个头盔。”（给他看。）

En: Well, there are these gauntlets... (Show him.)
Zh: “呃，这儿有副铁手套……”（给他看。）

En: I'm not purchasing any more clothing at the moment. He looks you up and down quickly. "And certainly not expensive armour that's liable to bring mercenaries to my doorstep."
Zh: “我目前不打算再多买衣服了……”他快速地上下打量了你一番。“肯定更不会买这么昂贵的盔甲了，要不很容易把雇佣兵引上门的。”

En: More than sure. What else have you got?
Zh: “非常确定。你还有什么？”

En: He steps back, away from the glass. "Oh, no, no, no, no. I don't need blood-thirsty mercenaries knocking on my door looking for their missing toys."
Zh: 他后退一步，离得玻璃远远地。“哦，不不不不。我可不想那些嗜血的雇佣兵来我这里找他们丢失的玩具。”

En: Besides, this gear costs a fortune -- there's no one in Martinaise with that kind of money.
Zh: “再说，这个装备很昂贵——马丁内斯可没人掏得起这个价。”

En: It feels like he would have accepted the risk if he hoped to profit from it. It's not greed, though. Deep down, he's a gambler -- provided the stakes are sufficiently interesting.
Zh: 如果他希望从中获利的话，似乎就会担下这个风险。不过，这并不叫贪婪。他的内心深处是一个赌徒——只要赌注足够有趣就行了。

En: (Step closer.) "Sir, could you take off your sunglasses? I'd like to check your eyes."
Zh: （靠近一些。）“先生，你可以取下自己的墨镜吗？我想直接看着你的眼睛。”

En: Why on earth? He staggers away from the glass, but quickly recomposes himself. "These are prescription. I can't really see without them."
Zh: “为什么？”玻璃后面，他的脚步有些蹒跚，但很快调整好了自己。“这是医用的，没有它们我真的看不见。”

En: Do you have any recordings of the Col Do Ma Ma Daqua?
Zh: “你有哈库多玛达塔的录音吗？”

En: The Col Do Ma Ma Daqua? He looks at you with astonishment. "Sorry, I would never have guessed that you were that interested in ultrasonic sounds... or *birds*, for that matter."
Zh: “哈库多玛达塔？”他惊讶地看着你。“抱歉，我绝对猜不到你居然会对超声波感兴趣……或者就此而言，是*鸟*。”

En: I've been really into all kinds of art lately. Experimental things, you know.
Zh: “我最近真的对各种类型的艺术都很感兴趣。实验性的东西，你懂得。”

En: I've been thinking of quitting the RCM and becoming a cryptozoologist.
Zh: “我在考虑要不要退出RCM，当一个神秘动物学家。”

En: I have hobbies. Just like all normal people. Because I'm a normal person.
Zh: “像所有普通人一样，我也有自己的兴趣爱好。因为我也是个普通人。”

En: Well, I wouldn't have guessed that you know what the Col Do Ma Ma Daqua is.
Zh: “呃，我没想到你居然会知道哈库多玛达塔是什么。”

En: That's fair. I just... It's been a long time since my Col Do Ma Ma Daqua hunting days, he responds with a thin smile. "Once knew a group of young musicians who decided they didn't want to play music anymore and started looking for all kinds of interesting sounds instead..."
Zh: “当然清楚。我只是……自从搜寻哈库多玛达塔的日子过去之后，我已经很久没有听到这个名字了，”他微笑着回应到。“我以前认识一群年轻的音乐家，他们决定以后不玩音乐，而是寻找各种各样有趣的声音……”

En: Oh, I didn't mean to imply otherwise... Well, it's been a while since I've gone hunting for the Col Do Ma Ma Daqua myself...
Zh: “哦，我不是想暗示什么其他的……呃，我已经很久没有去搜寻哈库多玛达塔了……”

En: How interesting! Well, it's been a while since I've gone hunting for the Col Do Ma Ma Daqua...
Zh: “真是太有意思了！呃，我已经很久没有去搜寻哈库多玛达塔了……”

En: What an interesting development! Well, it's been a while since I've gone hunting for the Col Do Ma Ma Daqua...
Zh: “真是有趣的发展！呃，我已经很久没有去搜寻哈库多玛达塔了……”

En: Once knew a group of young musicians who decided they didn't want to play music anymore and started looking for all kinds of interesting sounds instead...
Zh: “我以前认识一群年轻的音乐家，他们决定以后不玩音乐，而是寻找各种各样有趣的声音……”

En: Get to the point. Do you have any recordings now?
Zh: “言归正传。所以你这儿有录音吗？”

En: This was before... you know. Lost touch with them after all of that.
Zh: “这是在那个之前的……你明白吧。在那一切发生之后，我就失去跟它们的联系了。”

En: We thought we did! We got together all these recordings of unusual sound patterns, compared them... He chuckles. "Cut them up and combined them into the 'Simfonia Col Do Ma Ma Daqua.'"
Zh: “我们以为找到了！我们一起录过很多种不寻常的声音模式，然后对比……”他咯咯笑了起来。“剪成片段，然后再组合成‘哈库多玛达塔交响乐’。”

En: And did you ever find it?
Zh: “那你们有没有找到它？”

En: That sounds so cool.
Zh: “听起来真酷啊。”

En: Cool or not... One of them was obsessed with recording the Col Do Ma Ma Daqua -- and he was one of those passionate people who know a lot about all kinds of strange things, so he got the rest of us to join in his search...
Zh: “酷不酷不好说……他们之中有一个人沉迷于录制哈库多玛达塔的声音——而且他是个很有激情的人，很了解各种各样奇怪的东西。就这样，他让其他人都加入到自己的研究中了……”

En: He purses his lips. "I'm not sure. I wasn't even sure about our old recordings, which I no longer have... But I do have a tape with some ultrasonic sounds that *might* be what you're looking for. Would you like to hear?" He starts rummaging through some tapes behind the counter.
Zh: 他撅起嘴唇。“我不确定。我甚至都不太确定我们之前的旧录音里有没有……不过我确实有一盘超声波的磁带，里面*也许*有你要找的东西。你想听听看吗？”说完，他开始在柜台后的磁带里翻找起来。

En: Can I listen to it?
Zh: “我能听听吗？”

En: Hm, that's not very scientific.
Zh: “嗯，这好像不太科学。”

En: That really wasn't the point. After the 'Simfonia' we moved onto the sounds of office supplies or something like that... and I doubt any of us would have been any good at pushing papers.
Zh: “那不是重点。在‘交响乐'之后，我们转移到办公用品之类的声音上面……我怀疑我们当中有没有人擅长文书工作。”

En: Unfortunately, I don't have any recordings from my... old life. None at all! But I do have a tape with some ultrasonic sounds that *might* be what you're looking for. He starts rummaging through some tapes behind the counter.
Zh: “可惜，我没有任何关于我……过去生活的记录。一点也没有！不过我确实有一盘超声波的磁带，里面*也许*有你要找的东西。你想听听看吗？”说完，他开始在柜台后的磁带中翻找起来。

En: Do we really have time for this? the lieutenant whispers to you.
Zh: “我们真有这个闲功夫吗？”警督低语到。

En: Spin that bird song!
Zh: “播放鸟鸣之歌吧！”

En: Yes, it's absolutely necessary for my research.
Zh: “是的，对于我的研究来说，绝对是必要的。”

En: Nah, that's okay. It's just that I heard the Col Do Ma Ma Daqua outside the Whirling, and I wanted to make sure I wasn't the only one... Let's talk about something else now.
Zh: “不了，没事的。只是我刚在飞旋旅社外面听到了哈库多玛达塔的声音，想确定我不是唯一能听到的人……现在，我们聊聊别的吧。”

En: He looks at you oddly. "Alright," he says, putting the tapes back reluctantly.
Zh: 他怪异地看着你。“好吧，”他一边回答，一边不情愿地把磁带放了回去。

En: He dusts off a case, then takes out the tape and places it on the tape player. "This recording comes from down the coast... Wasn't looking to record anything specific -- just left a recording device there one morning."
Zh: 他掸掉磁带盒上的灰尘，把磁带取出来，然后放进录音机里。“这个录音是在海岸边收集到的……当时不是刻意要去录什么特别的东西——只是某天早上在那里留了一台录音设备。”

En: Keep in mind, I have to slow this one down enough to make sounds well over 200 kHz  audible to the human ear... It will be... strange. He switches on the tape player. The speakers begin to emit a low hum....
Zh: “记住，我必须放慢一点，这样才能保证人类的耳朵能够听到超过200千赫的声音……会有一点……奇怪。”他按下录音机。扬声器开始发出一阵低沉的嗡嗡声……

En: Please... He throws you a long-suffering look. "No more experimental listening while we're on this case."
Zh: “拜托……”他向你投来一个隐忍的眼神。“我们还在调查案子的时候，能不能不要再搞什么听力实验了。”

En: He switches off the tape player. "You know, now that I've listened to it on these new speakers -- it's *not* the Col Do Ma Ma Daqua. Wrong patterns, wrong... photons. Probably some insect trying to sing higher than its predators can hear. Still -- fascinating, aren't they, early morning sounds?"
Zh: 他关掉收音机。“你知道吗，现在用这些新的扬声器一听——好像*不是*哈库多玛达塔。模式不对，光子辐射……也不对。也许是某种昆虫试着发出高亢的声音，好让它的捕食者听不见。不过——清晨的声音还是很迷人，不是吗？”

En: That's an illusion. Your breathing is just as it was and will remain that way unless you start panicking.
Zh: 那是个幻觉。你的呼吸还是一如既往，除非你开始恐慌，否则还是会维持原样。

En: There's a growing sense of dread: the sound is coming from inside you but also surrounding you. It feels as though someone is standing just outside your range of vision and watching you, doing this to you...
Zh: 你突然感觉到越来越恐惧：那个声音来自你的内心深处，但也同样将你环绕其中。感觉好像有人就站在你的视线范围之外，观察着你，对你做着这样的事情……

En: As the hum grows louder, modulating, but always unnaturally, uncomfortably low, like it's coming not from the speakers, but also from inside your chest. Breathing is becoming difficult...
Zh: 嗡嗡声越来越大，就算调制过，也一直低的那么不自然，让人感觉很不舒服；就好像它不仅是从扬声器，还是从你的胸腔内部发出来的。呼吸开始变得困难……

En: Not good, not good, you're about to start suffocating, you have to stop this...
Zh: 不好，不太妙，你要窒息了，必须阻止这一切……

En: Gulls and skuas. But shhhh. He raises a pointer finger and inclines his head toward the speakers. A new, very high-pitched, shivering sound emerges amidst the others.
Zh: “海鸥和贼鸥。但是，嘘。”他举起食指，把头靠向扬声器。一种新的，非常尖锐的，颤抖的声音从其他声音中涌现出来。

En: He nods to you reassuringly just as more diverse, higher-pitched sounds, some random, some appearing to form patterns, hit your eardrums.
Zh: 他朝你安慰地点点头，，紧接着，一阵更加多变，更加尖锐的声音击中了你的鼓膜，它们有的很随机，有的则似乎是有规律的。

En: Sea birds, most likely. Gulls and such.
Zh: 很可能是海鸟。海鸥啊之类的。

En: Seagulls?
Zh: “海鸥？”

En: What's that?
Zh: “那是什么？”

En: And skuas. But shhhh. He raises a pointer finger and inclines his head toward the speakers. A new, high-pitched, shivering sound.
Zh: “还有贼鸥。但是，嘘。”他举起食指，把头靠向扬声器。有一种新的、尖锐的、颤抖的声音。

En: That's it! The signal in the noise! The "thin whisper"...
Zh: 就是它！噪音中的信号！“微弱的轻语”……

En: The low range of sounds is easier to handle with a focal point, but still troubling. You are mesmerized by the sounds, but also feel nausea welling up as the motif continues, then begins to recede, dissolving in what must be the sound of water lapping at the bank...
Zh: 低音域的声音更容易用焦点处理，不过还是很让人烦心。你被声音迷住了，不过随着声音的继续，那股恶心感还在不断向上翻涌；接着，它开始慢慢褪去，溶解在海浪拍打岸边的声音里……

En: I'm looking for something that plays a tape. For police business.
Zh: “我在找可以播放磁带的东西，警察查案要用。”

En:
