The boombox I bought... it should play this tape, right?	“我买的这个手提音箱……应该能播这盘磁带，对吧？”
0x0100008F0000280D	Dialogue Text	PAWNSHOP / ROY	Bird's Nest Roy	Of course. It's in working order still, isn't it? Just pick your tape and set it spinning. It all starts with the tape."
Zh: “当然。它还能正常工作，不是吗？只要挑好磁带，然后放进去。一切都是从磁带开始的。”

En: A tape? We have boomboxes for sale that will play it. Check the shelves.
Zh: “一盘磁带？我们这儿有手提音箱卖，应该能播那个。自己去架子上看看吧。”

En: Yeah, we still have those boomboxes on the shelves.
Zh: “是的，架子上还有些手提音箱。”

En: You're not telling me everything about the current Big Wig.
Zh: “关于现在的这位大人物，你还有有些隐瞒吧。”

En: There's more to his animosity toward the Union Boss.
Zh: 他对工会老大的敌意还有更深层的原因。

En: He sighs. "Tiphaine Holly, the previous head of the Union, was rather *more* pleasant. Some of us around here... well, we've never understood why she didn't run for a second term. Or where she went after withdrawing from politics. It was all very strange."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“蒂费纳·霍利，前任工会领袖，要*更*讨人喜欢一些。我们这里有些人……呃，我们一直不理解她为什么没有竞选连任，也不知道她退出政坛后去了哪里。一切都太奇怪了。”

En: I heard the forewoman before Evrart disappeared.
Zh: “我听说艾弗拉特之前的女工头消失了。”

En: Yes, it was all very strange. That forewoman was... more pleasant than Evrart. He shrugs. "But I guess it's all the same in the end..."
Zh: “是的，一切都太奇怪了。那个女工头……比艾弗拉特要和蔼可亲多了。”他耸耸肩。“但是我猜，最后结局都一样……”

En: Officer, the flashlight please.
If you don't mind, please put the flashlight away. Some of us have photonic sensitivities.
Oh right, sorry about that.
That's the pyrho talking -- there is no flashlight.
Put the flashlight away, officer. Having it is a privilege, not a right.
[Leave.]
'Photonic sensitivity' is not generally acknowledged by the medical establishment.
Textbook narcomaniac behaviour.
Officer please, I'm serious.
I do appreciate it.
No, this is my thing. (Keep holding the flashlight.)
Yeah, man...
Zh: “是啊，伙计……”

En: The light... wow.
Zh: “这个灯……哇哦。”

En: Radiowaves...
Zh: “无线电波……”

En: Like... yeah.
Zh: “就像是……是啊。”

En: ... yeah...
Welcome to the pawnshop!
Zh: “欢迎来到当铺！”

En: If you want to pawn... you're here...
Zh: “如果你想典当的话……你已经在这里了……”

En: You have to admit, it's rather clever, what he's done with it. He pauses, studying the light.
Zh: 他停下来，研究了一下街灯，然后补充到：“你必须承认，他做的这一切，真的挺聪明的。”

En: Okay. That checks out.
Zh: 好吧。那个已经检验过了。

En: A wise investment. I agree. The best part is that you *can't* carry it around -- I would really dislike it if you could.
Zh: “一项聪明的投资。我同意。最棒的地方是你*不能*带着它到处跑——如果你可以的话，我真的会很反感的。”

En: Leave your street light be for now. [Leave.]
Zh: 暂时离开你的街灯。[离开。]

En: Let's talk about this trade then, if you're happy with shares? (Start hustling.)
Zh: “我们来谈谈这笔交易吧，你对这些股票还满意吗？”（态度强硬起来。）

En: More than happy. Like I said, I value the street light at 700 reál. You decide how many of your shares that gets me.
Zh: “当然满意。我刚刚说了，这盏路灯价值700雷亚尔。你自己决定要给我多少股票吧。”

En: More than happy. I value the street light at 700 reál. You decide how many of your shares that gets me.
Zh: “当然满意。这盏路灯价值700雷亚尔。你自己决定要给我多少股票吧。”

En: A twentieth of your shares is a sensible offer. Very divisible as far as numbers go.
Zh: 拿出你股票的二十分之一比较合理，你也能够接受。

En: How does a twentieth sound to you?
Zh: “二十分之一的股票如何？”

En: I need time to mull this over. (Go back.)
Zh: “我需要时间好好考虑一下。”（回去。）

En: As *little* as possible. We only sell shares if we're getting something of *more value* for them. Shares are *premium* goods. THINK BIG BUSINESS!
Zh: 当然是越*少*越好。只有遇到比股票*更有价值*的东西，才会把它们卖掉。股票可是会*溢价*商品啊。多考虑考虑大生意！

En: I'll give you a third of my shares for it.
Zh: “我可以给你三分之一的股票。”

En: Of course, officer. There's a tiny hint of distress in his voice. "It's your decision."
Zh: “当然可以，警官。”他的语气中有些悲伤。“你自己决定吧。”

En: A *third*? Are you sure?
Zh: “*三分之一*？你确定？”

En: Hm, that's a reasonable offer. He raises a curled finger to his chin, looking as thoughtful as he can.
Zh: “嗯，合情合理。”他翘起一根手指托住下巴，陷入思考状。

En: He gulps. "That's... not enough."
Zh: 他一时语塞。“那……自然是不够的。”

En: I can't go higher than a few shares.
Zh: “给你几股，不能再多了。”

En: Whoa, whoa, whoa! What are you doing? That street light isn't worth anywhere near that much. Reel it in, moneybags. Or do you like being bankrupt?
Zh: 哇，哇，哇！你在干什么？那盏路灯根本不值那么多钱。收手吧，大富翁。难道你想破产吗？

En: Yes, I'm sure.
Zh: “嗯，我确定。”

En: Actually, no.
Zh: “其实吧，还是算了。”

En: Alright, you have a deal. The street light is all yours. His shoulders relax as if a load has been lifted off them. "Pleasure doing business with you."
Zh: “好，成交。这盏路灯是你的了。”他的肩膀放松下来，如释重负。“交易愉快。”

En: Take your time.
Zh: “那请便吧。”

En: Take it or leave it. Makes no odds to me.
Zh: “接不接受随你便。反正我无所谓。”

En: Forget it, then. I'll get more for the light in cash. Unless you want to reconsider?
Zh: “那就算了吧。这盏灯要是以现金出售的话我还能赚更多呢。除非你再考虑一下？”

En: Look, I was told that these shares will *double* in value in five years' time.
Zh: “有人和我说，这些股票的价值在五年内会*翻倍*。”

En: But I want to sell them *now*. He tilts his head slightly to consider this new information.
Zh: “可是我*现在*就想把它们卖了。”他歪着脑袋思索着。

En: It's the 700 reál modified street light you traded for shares.
Zh: 这是一台价值700雷亚尔的改造街灯，你用股票将它买了下来。

En: The pole has been carefully cut, the wiring redone and attached to a standard indoor plug. The light buzzes faintly but persistently.
Zh: 灯杆被小心地割断了，电线重新布置过，连在了一个标准的室内插头上。电灯发出微弱但却持久的嗡嗡声。

En: Look at the light in your life.
Zh: 看着你生命中的明灯。

En: She's going to *love* it.
Zh: 她会*喜欢*它的。

En: Read the sign.
Zh: 阅读标签。

En: See you later, sunshine. [Leave.]
Zh: “再见了，阳光。”[离开。]

En: It reads: 'SOLD TO THE DISCO COP FOR SHARES IN M-K MERCURY GROUP.' The words are written with an even kerning in bold, precise letters.
Zh: 上面用加粗的字体写道：‘以M-K水银集团的股票出售给迪斯科警察。’

En: Looks surprisingly official for a small-time trader. Some of your big-biz flair must be rubbing off on him.
Zh: 这对一个小贩来说可是过于正式了点。你的经商头脑一定也影响到他了。

En: So how do I get this light out of here? (Proceed.)
Zh: “我要怎么把这灯抬出去呢？”（继续。）

En: Some extra shares would go a long way to helping him out of debt. He deserves a break.
Zh: 手头多些股票能帮他缓解一下债务危机。他需要好好休息下了。

En: Hm? He surfaces from a daydream. "Oh, right. That's... good," he replies, unsure of what you said.
Zh: “嗯？”他回过神来。“哦，对。那……挺好的，”他回复道，但并不知道你刚才说了什么。

En: I've got big plans for this light. Your shop's gonna be a landmark one day.
Zh: “关于这盏灯，我有个好主意。你的店将来会成为地标的。”

En: He has an idea of what he wants but he's not giving it away. Sign of a veteran dealer -- able to withstand negotiations over minuscule amounts for *days* or even *weeks*.
Zh: 他很明白自己想要什么，而他也不会轻易放弃。为了多赚一点钱，能够花*几天*，甚至是*几周*时间去议价，这就是商场老手。

En: Something lifted in him when he saw those shares. He *needs* as many as possible -- even if he won't admit it.
Zh: 他见到那些股票的时候，似乎相当振奋。虽然他不愿意承认，但一定是非常*需要*这些股票，而且越多越好。

En: Alright, you have a deal. The street light is all yours. His shoulders relax as if a load has been lifted off them. "Pleasure doing business with you."
Zh: “好吧，成交。这盏路灯是你的了。”他的肩膀放松下来，如释重负。“交易愉快。”

En: This is all for show. He's already made his mind up.
Zh: 这都是做做样子罢了。他早就拿定主意了。

En: The pose! The power! Yessssss, drag this part out and reel in your audience, darling. See if they will crack under the suspense.
Zh: 姿势要对！气势要狠！没错，就是咄咄逼人，亲爱的。看看对方会不会在焦虑中动摇。

En: Alright, alright. That'll have to do. His shoulders relax slightly. "The street light is all yours."
Zh: “行吧行吧。也只能这样了。”他的肩膀稍稍放松了一些。“这盏路灯是你的了。”

En: Or has another light shining on the ceiling caught his eye? It's hard to tell.
Zh: 难道天花板上的另一盏灯吸引了他的注意力？这很难说。

En: Who is?
Zh: 谁啊？

En: (Don't ask.)
Zh: （不询问。）

En: Silence...
Zh: 沉默……

En: Nothing like a good business deal to loosen the joints, is there? (Rotate your shoulders.)
Zh: “没什么比做一笔好买卖更让人放松了，对吧？”（活动肩膀。）

En: (Ready your hands on your pistol.)
Zh: （把手放在手枪上。）

En: How can I trust that you won't sell it to someone else?
Zh: “我怎么能相信你不会把它卖给别人呢？”

En: Tell you what... He bends down and disappears behind the counter.
Zh: “听我说……”他弯下腰躲到柜台后边。

En: But... why? It's a perfectly wonderful light. Besides, he points to the sign on his booth, "there are no returns or exchanges for items purchased in this shop."
Zh: “可是……为什么呢？这盏灯那么好。而且，”他指着货架旁边的标志，“本店售出的所有商品，概不退换。”

En: I want my shares back then.
Zh: “我想要回我的股票。”

En: That's alright, I can keep it here for you until you find one.
Zh: “没事的，我帮你把东西留着，等你找到了再说。”

En: It is quite heavy. You're going to need a truck.
Zh: “它挺重的。你需要找辆货車。”

En: But... I don't have a truck.
Zh: “但是……我没有货車。”

En: Hey! Where'd you go?
Zh: “喂！你要去哪儿？”

En: Did he just do a runner? He can't get away that easily. Try calling out to him.
Zh: 他就这样逃掉了吗？他才没那么容易跑得掉呢。试着叫住他。

En: Don't panic. I'm sure he's just doing his shoelaces up. Something like that.
Zh: 别慌张。他肯定是在系鞋带之类的吧。

En: What if he's grabbing a GUN?!
Zh: 要是他去拿枪了该怎么办？！

En: He doesn't quite catch your meaning. "Oh... sure." He tries to mimic your rotational action but just ends up shrugging.
Zh: 他好像不太明白你的意思。“哦……好吧”。他本想着要学你一样活动肩膀，可最后也只是耸了耸肩。

En: The lieutenant catches your movements and meets your eyes with a look of confusion.
Zh: 警督留意到你的动作，一脸疑惑地看着你。

En: (Pretend to browse the shop from where you're stood.)
Zh: （假装浏览店里的商品。）

En: The pawnshop owner uprights himself back into view with the stiffness of an old ironing board. "Here we go," he says, holding a piece of cardboard out at you.
Zh: 当铺老板挺直身板，仿佛一个老旧的烫衣板。“给，”他递给你一块纸板。

En: How does it feel to be a *shareholder*?
Zh: “当*股东*的感觉如何？”

En: Yeah, good. But I won't be holding onto these shares for long. He gives you a false grin. "I've got to pay those debts off."
Zh: “挺不错的。不过这些股票我也存不了太久。”他朝你挤出一个假笑。“我还得把债还清。”

En: Yeah, good. But I won't be holding onto these shares for long. He gives you a false grin.
Zh: “挺不错的。不过这些股票我也存不了太久。”他朝你挤出一个假笑。

En: Got some big purchases planned, have you?
Zh: “你要大买一场吗？”

En: Let's just say there's some long overdue business I've got to deal with.
Zh: “我还有些遗留问题要处理。”

En: Silence...
Zh: 保持沉默……

En: It'll have to do for now.
Zh: “现在只能这样了。”

En: I like your thinking.
Zh: “你的想法挺不错。”

En: He watches you attach the sign to the street light using the sticky tack he put on the back of it. "There you go. Beautiful job."
Zh: 他看着你用标签后面的粘合剂把它贴到路灯上边。“好了，真不错。”

En: Great idea! One of your physical assets *on display* for the public. Maybe this was a good idea after all. We could sell tickets for people to come see it, turn this into a little money churner...
Zh: 好主意！这可是你能向大家*展示*的物质资产啊。没准这真是个好主意呢。我们可以售票让大家来欣赏它，把它变成我们的摇钱树……

En: But let's come back to that idea later. There's easier money to be made elsewhere. Just get that sign stuck on there for now.
Zh: 不过问题以后再讨论吧。从别的地方赚钱没准更容易。现在先把标签贴上吧。

En: What's that?
Zh: “那是什么？”

En: (Take it.)
Zh: （收下。）

En: He hands you the cardboard and you notice there's something written on it. "Stick this to the light and people will know that it's *yours*."
Zh: 他递给你一块纸板，上面的内容引起了你的注意。“把这个贴在灯上，大家就都知道这东西是*你的*了。”

En: It's a sign that declares you the owner of the street light. Stick it on and people will know that it's *yours*.
Zh: “这个标签可以说明你是路灯的主人。把这个贴在灯上，大家就都知道这东西是*你的*了。”

En: He hands you the sign then watches you attach it to the street light using the sticky tack he put on the back. "There you go. Beautiful job."
Zh: 他把标签递给你，看着你用标签后面的粘合剂把它贴到路灯上边。“好了，真不错。”

En: (Search for something you can defend yourself with.)
Zh: （寻找用来自卫的东西。）

En: You tense up as the pawnshop owner uprights himself back into view. There isn't a gun in his hands -- or anywhere else on him. Instead, he holds out a piece of cardboard. "Here we go."
Zh: 你紧张万分，当铺老板重新出现在了你的视线里。可他手中——甚至任何地方没有枪的影子。他递给你一块纸板。“拿去吧。”

En: Phew. (Relax.) "I thought you were grabbing a gun."
Zh: “呼。”（放松。）“我以为你在掏枪呢。”

En: Oh, nothing like that. He is completely unfazed by your statement. "Just this sign that declares you the owner of the street light. Stick it on and people will know that it's *yours*."
Zh: “哦，怎么可能。”他并没有在意你说的话。“这个标签可以说明你是路灯的主人。把这个贴在灯上，大家就都知道这东西是*你的*了。”

En: He's trying to cut you with its sharp edge!
Zh: 他想用纸板锋利的边缘划伤你！

En: He's *really* not. Just take it off him.
Zh: 他*真的*不会。快拿过来吧。

En: Careful where you point that thing. It's sharp!
Zh: “你拿这东西的时候别乱指。很锋利的！”

En: I assure you it's not. He runs a finger along its edge. "See? Not a scratch. Now, please, take it and stick it on the street light. That way people will know it's *yours*."
Zh: “保证没有。”他用手指拂过纸板边缘。“瞧见没？一点划痕都没有。好了，拿着它，然后贴到路灯上。这样大家都知道这玩意儿是*你的*了。”

En: Let's talk about trading those shares again.
Zh: “我们再谈谈股票的事情吧。”

En: Yes please, he says eagerly.
Zh: “好啊，”他满脸期待地说道。

En: Uh, comes a voice from behind the glass. "One quick question. How do I get hold of my shares?"
Zh: “啊，”从玻璃后边传来一个声音。“我想问个简单的问题。我要怎么持股呢？”

En: I'll get my Personal Brand Strategist to sort the legal work out with you soon. (Mumble.) "Once I find another one..."
Zh: “我会让我的个人品牌战略师和你对接法务事宜。”

En: I'll get my Personal Brand Strategist to sort the legal work out with you soon.
Zh: “我会让我的个人品牌战略师和你对接法务事宜。”（咕哝。）“如果我能再找一个的话……”

En: I'll get my Personal Brand Strategist to sort the legal work out with you. (Mumble.) "Once I have one..."
Zh: “我会让我的个人品牌战略师和你对接法务事宜。”（咕哝。）“如果我有的话……”

En: Oh, right. Perfect. Thank you.
Zh: “哦，那太好了。谢谢你。”

En: A bargain?! No, it's not! He's trying to sweet-talk you into buying trash!
Zh: 物美价廉？不，不是的！他是想用甜言蜜语哄骗你买垃圾！

En: A typical Martinaise street light sits among assorted floor and table lamps.
Zh: 一个标准的马丁内斯街灯坐落在地板和各色台灯之间。

En: Let your gaze run over the street light.
Zh: 快速打量一下街灯。

En: This is the street light you want to trade?
Zh: “这就是你想用来和我交易的路灯吗？”

En: Is that a street light?
Zh: “那是街灯吗？”

En: Where did you get this?
Zh: “你从哪里弄来的？”

En: The light pole has been carefully cut, and the wiring has been redone and attached to a standard indoor plug. The light buzzes faintly but persistently.
Zh: 灯杆被小心地割断了，电线重新布置过，连在了一个标准的室内插头上。电灯发出微弱但却持久的嗡嗡声。

En: Yes, officer. As you can see, it's in perfect working order.
Zh: “是的，警官。相信你也看到了，它的运行状态非常良好。”

En: We're not here to investigate the theft of city property. He leans in so the pawnbroker doesn't hear him.
Zh: 他靠了过来，不让当铺老板听到他的话语。“我们来这里可不是为了调查城市财产失窃案的。”

En: The light's buzzing resembles the purring of a cat. It seems very much at home here.
Zh: 灯光的嗡嗡声就像猫咪发出的呼噜声一样，让这里有了一种家的感觉。

En: It was brought to me to be altered.
Zh: “那个是拿过来给我改造的。”

En: His manner is casual, but his speech is careful, measured. He wants you to know that he has nothing to hide.
Zh: 他的态度很随意，但是他的言辞十分小心谨慎。他想让你知道，自己没什么好隐瞒的。

En: How much have you been asking for this -- in cash?
Zh: “这个你要价多少——现金支付的话？”

En: How much for the street light?
Zh: “这个街灯多少钱？”

En: Seven hundred reál. A bargain, I dare say.
Zh: “700雷亚尔。我敢说，已经很物美价廉了。”

En: Are you out of your mind? There's one just like that on every corner!
Zh: “你疯了吗！？这玩意每个街角都有一模一样的。”

En: Even taking into account the risk of obtaining the light, that seems a bit steep....
Zh: “就算考虑到弄到这个街灯的风险，好像也有点太夸张了吧……”

En: There's nothing else you need to know about the light. [Leave.]
Zh: 关于这个灯，你已经没什么需要知道的了。[离开。]

En: The light has undergone three transformations, and every transformation, large or small, has a price tag. He rests his elbows on the counter and brings his face closer to the glass.
Zh: 他把胳膊肘靠在柜台上，脸贴近了玻璃窗。“这个灯经过了三次改造，每一次改造，不论大小，都有个价格。”

En: There's also the matter of rewiring. But the most important transformation is the light's placement among ordinary *indoor* fixtures, which has adjusted its morphological field. The light became suitable for use inside the home just a few days ago.
Zh: “还有重新配线的事呢。不过，最重要的改造是把灯布置在普通的*室内*固定装置上，相当于调整了它的形态场。几天前，这盏灯才刚刚变得能在室内使用。”

En: The light has been removed and rewired -- that's two *transformations*. But what about the third?
Zh: 灯被拆掉，然后重新配线——这是两次*改造*。那第三次呢？

En: I get how sawing it off and redoing the wiring could add to the cost, but what else?
Zh: “把它锯开，然后重新配线确实会增加花费，但是还有什么呢？”

En: What do you mean, *transformations*?
Zh: “你说*改造*是什么意思？”

En: Well, there are the costs of removal and rewiring. He throws you a look of weary patience.
Zh: 他露出一副不耐烦的样子。“呃，拆解和重新配线都是要成本的。”

En: The most important transformation is the light's placement among ordinary *indoor* fixtures, which has adjusted its morphological field. The light became suitable for use inside the home just a few days ago. He throws you a look of weary patience.
Zh: 他露出一副不耐烦的样子。“最重要的改造是把灯布置在普通的*室内*固定装置上，相当于调整了它的形态场。几天前，这盏灯才刚刚变得能在室内使用。”

En: But the most important transformation is the light's placement among ordinary *indoor* fixtures, which has adjusted its morphological field. The light became suitable for use inside the home just a few days ago.
Zh: “但是，最重要的改造是把灯布置在普通的*室内*固定装置上，相当于调整了它的形态场。几天前，这盏灯才刚刚变得能在室内使用。”

En: I want that street light. Here's 700.
Zh: “我想要那个街灯。给你700。”

En: This would make quite a statement in your living room.
Zh: 这个放在你的起居室效果一定很好。

En: Seven hundred sounds about right. I imagine it wasn't easy sawing off that street lamp.
Zh: “700听起来还好。我能想象的到，锯断那个街灯可不容易。”

En: Hey, I don't know where it came from, but it's not every day you get to buy and sell something so extraordinary...
Zh: “嘿，我不知道它是从哪里来的，不过你不是每天都能买卖这么特别的东西……”

En: But how am I going to take it with me?
Zh: “但是我该怎么带走呢？”

En: It is quite heavy. You're going to need a truck.
Zh: “它挺重的。你需要找辆货車。”

En: But... I don't have a truck.
Zh: “但是……我没有货車。”

En: That's alright, I can keep it here for you until you find one.
Zh: “没事的，我帮你把东西留着，等你找到了再说。”

En: Thanks, I appreciate it.
Zh: “谢谢，我很感激。”

En: I want my shares back.
Zh: “我想要回我的股票。”

En: I want my money back.
Zh: “我想要回我的钱。”

En: But... why? It's a perfectly wonderful light.
Zh: “但是……为什么？这不是一台完美又精妙的灯吗？”

En: Besides, he points to the sign on his booth, "there are no returns or exchanges for items purchased in this shop."
Zh: “再说了，”他指着小亭子旁边的标志，“本店售出的所有商品，概不退换。

En: Excellent choice. I'm almost sad to part with it.
Zh: “真是个优秀的选择。一想到要跟它分开，我都有点伤心了。”

En: Your eyes slide back to the 700 reál modified street light you traded for shares. The golden light seems to flicker ever so slightly, like a butterfly... It casts shadows on the sign.
Zh: 你的目光又转回用股票换来的价值700雷亚尔的改造路灯上。金色的光芒微弱地闪烁着，就像一只蝴蝶……在标签上投下了阴影。

En: It's the 700 reál modified street light you bought for some unknown reason.
Zh: 这是一台价值700雷亚尔的改造街灯，你因为某种不明原因把它买下来了。

En: It's no problem. Come back any time to look at it. It's *your* sawed off street light now.
Zh: “没问题。你可以随时回来看它。现在它是*你*的街灯了。”

En: Great. I guess I'm stuck with it. [Leave.]
Zh: 太好了。我猜。[离开。]

En: Is there still no way you'd give me my money back? I can't do anything with this light.
Zh: “你真的没可能把钱还我吗？这台灯对我来说一点用都没有。”

En: I'm sorry, there are no returns or exchanges for items purchased in this shop. Just enjoy it.
Zh: “很抱歉，本店售出的所有商品概不退换。你就好好享受它吧。”

En: Look at the light in your life.
Zh: 看着你生命中的明灯。

En: Still the same. The pole has been carefully cut, the wiring redone and attached to a standard indoor plug. The light buzzes faintly but persistently.
Zh: 还是一样。灯杆被小心地割断了，电线重新布置过，连在了一个标准的室内插头上。电灯发出微弱但却持久的嗡嗡声。

En: Makes you feel *rebellious*.
Zh: 让你有种*桀骜不驯*的感觉。

En: Street lights like this one were part of a renovation effort a few decades back. Sadly, the effort was abandoned before it was completed -- and so these lights still illuminate crumbling streets.
Zh: 像这种街灯是数十年前翻新工程的部分成果之一。可悲的是，工程还没完成就废弃了——所以这些崭新的街灯还在为破烂不堪的街道照明。

En: Fine. He nods decisively. "Four reál it is."
Zh: “好吧。”他决然地点点头。“那就4雷亚尔了。”

En: Stand on the tips of your toes to see more...
Zh: 踮起脚尖多看一点……

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: That's odd. Why doesn't he like music?
Zh: 奇怪。为什么他不喜欢音乐呢？

En: Hmm... maybe you should ditch music as well, get into these more *experimental* sounds he's describing?
Zh: 嗯……也许你也应该放弃音乐，尝试他描述的那种更有*实验性*的声音？

En: One especially catches your eye. Deep gold and amber plastic with a big old handle on top. A classic boombox that says: "STEREO 8 approved."
Zh: 有一个特别抓你眼球。深金和琥珀色的塑料，顶上有一个巨大的旧把手。一款经典的手提音箱，上面写着：“立体声8认证。”

En: What I really want to know is... could this device come handy in my police work?
Zh: “其实我想知道的是……我的警察工作用得着这个设备吗？”

En: If police work means playing tapes, sure. You can use it for that. Or any other time you'd need to play a tape.
Zh: “如果警察要播磁带的话，那当然了。你可以这么用。或者其他需要播磁带的时候。”

En: Like at a beach party. With sand and sun and seagulls dancing on the breeze.
Zh: 就像在海滩派对上。阳光，沙滩，还有海鸥在微风中舞蹈。

En: Got it. Back to this guy... (Tap the boombox on its speaker.)
Zh: “明白了。说回这个家伙……”（拍拍手提音箱的扬声器。）

En: He is a good one, that. Weather resistant.
Zh: “那家伙挺不错的，能防风防雨。”

En: The boomboxes wait on the shelves. And your boombox -- that gold and amber Harmon Wowshi -- stares at you longingly with its tape reel eyes.
Zh: 手提音箱在架子上等待着。而你的音箱——那台金色和琥珀色的哈蒙沃斯——用它的带卷眼热切地看着你。

En: You're right. It's really The End that I need it for: a Requiem For All Lifeforms.
Zh: “你说得对。只有在世界末日才需要它：为所有生命奉上一首安魂曲。”

En: Theoretically, yes. But we don't have time right now. It's generally: murder investigation first, *then* beach party.
Zh: “从理论上说，是的。但我们现在没有时间。一般是这样的：先调查凶杀案，*然后*才是海滩派对。”

En: Actually, it doesn't have to be a beach. With a boombox like that, I'll bring the party into the streets!
Zh: “其实，也不用非得是海滩。有了这种手提音箱，就算是街上也能开派对！”

En: (Turn to Kim.) "Theoretically: could I bring it to a beach party?"
Zh: （转向金。）“从理论上讲：我能把它带去海滩派对吗？”

En: Thanks for clearing that up, Roy.
Zh: “感谢你的澄清，罗伊。”

En: For sure. It's a consumer device with professional applications.
Zh: “当然。这是一台消费设备，拥有专业的应用程序。”

En: I don't like beaches. They accumulate too many photons, it's always chaos out there. But live your life, man. You can use it at a beach party if you want.
Zh: “我不喜欢海滩。那里会积攒太多的光子，总是一片混乱。但是好好活出你自己的人生吧，伙计。如果你想，就在海滩派对上用吧。”

En: You can play it anywhere you like. And I guess since you're are a police officer, no one can arrest you for it.
Zh: “你想在哪儿放都可以。我觉得既然你是个警察，应该没人会逮捕你吧。”

En: It'll play that, the man nods. "Any Requiem really. Also dirges. Madrigals. Chants. As long as it's on tape."
Zh: “那个也能播的，”男人点点头。“所有安魂曲都行。挽歌也可以。情歌。儿歌。只要磁带上有就行。”

En: A man like that in a neighbourhood like this? In a rickety house falling into the sea? There's fear in him. Use it to your advantage.
Zh: 在这样的街区，一个那样的男人？在一栋摇摇欲坠就快掉进海里的房子？他很害怕。你要好好利用这一点。

En: He seems like an *experimental* man. And what sonic experience is more experimental than the *Doorgunner Megamix*? Certainly he'd give you a discount if he knew you'd play that.
Zh: 他看起来像是个*实验性*的男人。还有什么声音体验能比*门枪手精选集*更有实验性的呢？如果他知道你要播那个，肯定会给你打折的。

En: Remember -- he doesn't like music. He likes *sounds*. The *Doorgunner Megamix* is his type of tape! Certainly he'd give you a discount if he knew you'd play something so *experimental*.
Zh: 记住——他不喜欢音乐。他喜欢*声音*。*门枪手精选集*就是他会喜欢的那种磁带！如果他知道你要播*实验性*的东西，肯定会给你打折的。

En: Just don't ask him for the smallest amount. You'll insult him. The others will work.
Zh: 只是不要问他最小的数目。这样对他是一种侮辱。其他的也许能行。

En: How does ten reál sound?
Zh: “10雷亚尔怎么样？”

En: Actually, yes. Let's. Eight is an even better number than twelve. Even more internal balance. He observes the light on the wall and adds: "Better integrity too."
Zh: “其实，可以吧。就这样吧。8这个数字比12更好一些。内在甚至更加平衡了。”他观察着墙上的灯光，然后补充到：“完整性也更好。”

En: Sounds like a ripoff, but okay. I can make it ten -- but no lower.
Zh: “听起来像是在盗窃，不过行吧。我可以降到10雷亚尔——不过不能再低了。”

En: Let's make it eight reál.
Zh: “要不就8雷亚尔吧。”

En: Aren't you afraid of *masked gunmen*? The roof falling in? Killer waves? Aircraft accidents? Botched surgeries? I'll buy it for four reál.
Zh: “难道你不害怕*戴面具的枪手*吗？屋顶塌下来？杀手浪潮？飞艇事故？搞砸了的外科手术？我出4雷亚尔。”

En: I'm not just doing *any* police work -- I've got *THE DOORGUNNER MEGAMIX* here! An era-defining work. Let's do four reál.
Zh: “我不只是在做什么*随便的*警察工作——我这里还有*门枪手精选集*！一件划时代的作品。要不就4雷亚尔吧。”

En: Let me hit you with this: four reál. Huh? Whaddya say?
Zh: “这样吧：4雷亚尔。怎么样？你觉得呢？”

En: No, not really. I expect momentous change at any moment... But it seems that *you* are. He almost sounds concerned and seems to weigh your offer.
Zh: “不，不算吧。我任何时候都在期待重要的变化……不过看起来这个变化是*你*。”他听起来几乎有些担心，好像在权衡你的价格。

En: He purses his lips. "Four reál is offensively low... but just this once. For the *musique concrète* cop."
Zh: 他努起嘴唇。“4雷亚尔低的让人讨厌……但仅此一次吧。为了*具象音乐*警察。”

En: I say: it's twelve reál. Four is a total ripoff. It's not even a number.
Zh: “我说了：12雷亚尔。4你完全就是盗窃吧，它根本就不算是个数字。”

En: All the old boomboxes are there, blinking and waiting to whirl. All but one: yours.
Zh: 所有老旧的手提音箱都在那里，眨巴着眼睛，等待着旋转。只有一个除外：你的那台。

En: What did I say? And what did you do? Damn it, man. You're buying it for twelve now, I guess.
Zh: 我说什么了？看看你做了什么？见鬼了吧，伙计。我猜现在你要花12雷亚尔了。

En: This is you. Gold and orange. A sunset suite.
Zh: 这就是你。金色和橙色。日落组曲。

En: Just make sure it works before you buy it.
Zh: 在买之前，只要确认它能工作就行。

En: I don't think the 'Doorgunner Megamix' will be good party material. Something about the name...
Zh: “我不觉得‘门枪手精选集’很适合派对。那个名字感觉有点……”

En: You're right, Kitsuragi. Work first. Party later.
Zh: “你说的对，曷城。先工作，后派对。”

En: Sounds like he's already heard the Requiem For All Lifeforms and wasn't that impressed.
Zh: 看来他已经听过献给所有生命的安魂曲了，而且也没有被打动。

En: That is the unofficial RCM motto, yes.
Zh: “那是非官方的RCM座右铭，没错。”

En: It's not, sadly.
Zh: 可悲的是，它不是。

En: Careful. You can only do this once.
Zh: 小心。你只能做一次。

En: Succcess!!! Sort of. Whatever gets the job done, right?
Zh: 成功！！！算是吧。只要能完成任务，对吧？

En: The boomboxes on the shelf look well-loved and well-travelled. Chipped, dented, they stare at you with the unblinking eyes of their tape reels.
Zh: 架子上的那些手提音箱看起来备受喜爱，而且去过很多地方。碎裂，凹陷，它们的带卷就像一双不会眨动的眼睛，死死地盯着你。

En: Shopkeep, this Stereo 8 approved machine here...
Zh: “老板，这台立体声8认证机器……”

En: Are you sure this is all in working order?
Zh: “你确定它真能正常工作吗？”

En: I want to buy that boombox.
Zh: “我想买那台手提音箱。”

En: That's all. [Leave.]
Zh: 就这样。[离开。]

En: ...is the Harmon Wowshi W02 -- made in Vesper, designed in Seol, he says. "Plays all reel-to-reel formats: 2mm, 8mm, 12mm. It's even got a little radio in there. It'll set you back 12 reál."
Zh: “……是哈蒙沃斯W02——西奥设计，维斯珀制造，”他回答到。“可以播放所有卷对卷格式：2毫米、8毫米、12毫米。里面甚至还有个小无线电。它会让你破费12雷亚尔。”

En: Absolutely. I've tested each one myself with recordings of speech, found sounds, and music from a variety of genres. Even though, he grimaces, "I don't really like music."
Zh: “当然了。每一台我都用演讲录音、唱片、还有各个流派的音乐亲自测试过。不过，”他做了个鬼脸，“我不太喜欢音乐。”

En: And here you are. Quality sound reproduction on the go. It'll play anything. Wherever. Turn *any* tape into a conversation of sounds and shapes.
Zh: “给你。高质量放声。它什么都能播。不论在哪里都可以。可以把*任何*磁带变成声音和形状的对话。”

En: Don't like music? What do you like then?
Zh: “不喜欢音乐？那你喜欢什么？”

En: The stuff I record myself -- silverware shaking in drawers as motorcars race by, nocturnal animals climbing on the roof, airship rotors, that kind of thing.
Zh: “我自己录的东西——汽車飞速驶过时，抽屉里银器摇晃的声音，爬上屋顶的夜行动物的声音，飞艇转子的声音，那一类的。”

En: Can I get a discount on this boombox? A *police* discount.
Zh: “这个手提音箱不能给我打个折吗？来个*警察*折扣。”

En: Can I just play a tape on one of the boomboxes real quick?
Zh: “我能不能用手提音箱播一盘磁带，很快就好？”

En: Sorry, man. I can't be giving out freebies. Never have, won't now.
Zh: “抱歉，伙计。我这里不提供免费赠品。之前从来没有过，现在也不会有。”

En: Fair enough, business man. Twelve it is.
Zh: “说的有道理，做生意嘛。那就12吧。”

En: Twelve is a good number. Good internal balance. He studies the light on the wall. "Twelve has integrity."
Zh: “12是个好数字。很好的内在平衡。”他研究着墙上的灯光。“12有完整性。”

En: A discount? I do have to keep the lights on, man. It's twelve reál.
Zh: “打折？我这里还要钱维持供电呢。12雷亚尔不改了。”

En: Thanks for the discount, man. Here's the money for the boombox.
Zh: “感谢你的折扣，伙计。这是买音箱的钱。”

En: Thanks for the discount, man. Here's the money for the boombox.
Zh: “感谢你的折扣，伙计。这是买音箱的钱。”

En: Thanks for the discount, man. Here's the money for the boombox.
Zh: “感谢你的折扣，伙计。这是买音箱的钱。”

En: I need to buy that boombox.
Zh: “我需要买下那台手提音箱。”

En: Okay, Kim. I'll just rock out with the pawnshop owner then.
Zh: “好的，金。那我就跟当铺老板一起摇滚吧。”

En: Okay Kim. I'll just rock out with Roy then.
Zh: “好的，金。那我就跟罗伊一起摇滚吧。”

En: The Headless FALN Rider!
Zh: “无头FALN骑士！”

En: Indeed. He immediately knows what you're talking about. "I think I even have his cap somewhere down here..." He looks for it. "A tiny miniature FALN cap to go with it. Yours for 50 cents."
Zh: “确实。”他立马就明白了你在说什么。“我觉得那下面好像还有他的帽子呢……”他翻找着。“一个配套的缩小版FALN帽子。给你的话，50分。”

En: The lieutenant studies the figurine with amusement.
Zh: 警督饶有兴致地研究着这些小雕像。

En: Cool rider, right? I knew we'd find something worthwhile here! Buy it!
Zh: 挺酷的骑士，对吧？我就知道我们能在这里找到值钱的东西！快把它买下来！

En: Wait, this looks like René, the old guy who was playing petanque.
Zh: “等等，这个看起来很像雷内，就是玩地掷球的那个老人。”

En: (Point at the knights on horseback.) "These are Franconigerian cavalry, right?"
Zh: （指向马背上的骑士。）“这些是弗兰考格斯骑兵，对吗？”

En: (Point at the knights on horseback.) "Who are they?"
Zh: （指向马背上的骑士。）“他们是谁？”

En: Franconigerian knights. He looks at the dusty figurines in the dim light. "I used to be very serious about these guys."
Zh: “弗兰考格斯骑兵。”他看着微弱的灯光下那尊灰扑扑的小雕像。“我以前对这些家伙特别用心。”

En: Mhm. I used to be very serious about my Franconigerian knights... He looks at the dusty figurines in the dim light.
Zh: “嗯。我以前对自己的弗兰考格斯骑士特别用心……”他看着黯淡灯光下满是灰尘的小雕像。

En: ...a long, long time ago.
Zh: ……很久，很久以前了。

En: This is what the loyalists looked like, yes. He inspects the figurine. "At first. Then they wised up and got camouflage."
Zh: “皇家士兵就是这个样子的，没错。”他仔细观察着小雕像。“一开始是。后来他们衰落了，穿上了伪装。”

En: That they do -- the uniforms are precisely the same. Some even have the same shape medals pinned to their chests.
Zh: 他们确实是——制服看上去几乎一模一样。有些士兵的胸口甚至钉着相同形状的勋章。

En: It is impossible to win against the cohorts of capital. Ask my friend Garte, whose bitch it made me.
Zh: “要战胜资本的军队是不可能的。问问我的朋友加尔特，我他妈是谁的走狗。”

En: I wish it was more *nuanced*. As it stands, I cannot comment.
Zh: “我希望它能更*细腻*一些。按照实际情况来说，我不会发表意见。”

En: Okay. He doesn't bat an eye. "I don't like either set too much, to be honest."
Zh: “好吧。”他不动声色。“说实话，我也不太喜欢这一套。”

En: He doesn't elaborate on what that means -- it's unlikely that he will.
Zh: 他没有详细说明那到底意味着什么——也不见得会说。

En: He doesn't elaborate on these 'wrong hands' -- it's unlikely that he ever will.
Zh: 他没有详细说明这个‘坏人手里’——他也不太可能会说。

En: Fifty cents. Bargain-priced! I'll throw in the tiny cap too -- I think he's looking for it, or something. That part of the story has many interpretations.
Zh: “50分。这价格很优惠了！那个小帽子也一起算上——我觉得他在找它，或者其他什么东西。故事的这个部分有很多种诠释。”

En: He lost his cap when he lost his head. Perhaps he's looking for the *head*?
Zh: 当他丢掉自己脑袋的时候，同时也弄丢了他的帽子。也许他在寻找*脑袋*呢？

En: Try to find something pretty and cool here. Then use it to win her back!
Zh: 尝试在这里找到些漂亮和酷炫的东西。然后用它把她赢回来！

En: Win 'her' back?
Zh: 把‘她’赢回来？

En: Yes! Buy something nice! A figurine.
Zh: 买点好东西！一个小雕像。

En: This sounds... off. You shouldn't trust this guy.
Zh: 这个听起来……不太对。你不应该相信这家伙。

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: (Look at Kim, then back at Roy.) "Sorry, what are we talking about again?"
Zh: （看看金，然后看向罗伊。）“抱歉，我们刚才说什么来着？”

En: Toy soldiers stand guard over bowls filled with bracelets, rings, and used cigarette lighters.
Zh: 玩具士兵们守卫着大碗，里面装着手镯、戒指和用过的香烟打火机。

En: You see rows of toy soldiers guarding the rest of the trinkets displayed on the table: some on horseback, others in rags, others yet in bright blue uniforms. All are stern and unyielding in their duty.
Zh: 你看见好几排玩具兵，守卫着桌子上的其他小物件：有些骑在马背上，有些衣衫褴褛，还有些穿着亮蓝色的制服。全都在认真履行自己的职责。

En: Inspect the knights on horseback.
Zh: 查看马背上的骑士。

En: Dig up a truly *cool* figurine in the box under the table.
Zh: 在桌子下面的箱子里，翻到一个真正*酷炫*的小雕像。

En: Dresscode: Extreme performance.
Zh: 穿着FALN裤子。

En: Dresscode: Rummager.
Zh: 穿着FALN球鞋。

En: Heard about FALN rider.
Zh: 听说过FALN骑士。

En: Standard Wirrâl Die.
Zh: 标准威勒尔骰子。

En: Everything you pick out seems faded, chipped, and sad somehow. Most of them are  just broken toys.
Zh: 你挑出来的所有东西似乎都有些褪色，残破，不知道怎么的，还有些悲哀。大部分都是些破玩具。

En: Inspect the blue uniforms.
Zh: 观察蓝色制服。

En: Inspect the figurines in rags.
Zh: 查看衣衫褴褛的小雕像。

En: Purchase the Headless FALN Rider and his cap.
Zh: 买下无头FALN骑士和他的帽子。

En: Big men on big horses clad in lamellar armour and carrying flintlocks. The kind that would mow down a line of enemy soldiers in the blink of an eye.
Zh: 一个身材高大、穿着鳞甲的人骑着高头大马，身上背着燧发枪。这类士兵可以在眨眼之间消灭一队敌军士兵。

En: These are Franconigerian cavalry, right?
Zh: “这些是弗兰考格斯骑兵，对吗？”

En: Yes! The man behind the glass takes says. "A little worn around the edges, but I'm pretty sure it's fully intact."
Zh: “是的！”玻璃窗后的男人回答道。“边缘稍微有点磨损，但我很肯定它还是完整的。”

En: Shopkeep, what are these?
Zh: “老板，这是什么？”

En: Why, what's this? A headless man riding a horse? A headless man wearing futuristic track suit trousers that say FALN!
Zh: 怎么了，这是什么？一个骑马的无头男？一个无头男子，穿着未来派的运动服裤子，上面还印着FALN的字样！

En: What is this? (Show it to the shopkeep.)
Zh: “这是什么？”（拿给当铺老板看）

En: Oh, that's the Headless FALN Rider.
Zh: “哦，那是无头FALN骑士。”

En: Of course. (Nod knowingly.)
Zh: “当然。”（故意点点头。）

En: *Who*?
There's been a lot of *interest* in that particular figurine. I had to hide it so it wouldn't fall into the wrong hands.
Zh: “很多人对那个特别的小雕像很*感兴趣*。我把它藏起来，就是不想让它落到坏人手里。”

En: The Headless FALN Rider. It's an urban legend -- about a man who rides the streets of Revachol sporting a FALN tracksuit. As you can see, he's missing his head. He points at the decapitated figurine.
Zh: “无头FALN骑士。那是一个都市传说——说的是一个穿着FALN运动服的男人，骑着马在瑞瓦肖的街道上到处游走。而且他没有头。”他指向没有脑袋的小雕像。

En: A classic set. The figurines represent the Fifth-century Franconigerian Cavalry.
Zh: “很经典的一套。这些小雕像代表5世纪的弗兰考格斯骑兵。”

En: The lieutenant studies the tiny figurine in your hand. "I've heard about it," he says with amusement, "I've heard the Headless FALN Rider rides a headless bull."
Zh: 警督研究着你手中的小雕像。“我听说过它，”他打趣着说到，“我听说无头FALN骑士的坐骑是一只无头公牛。”

En: Yes, there are several competing versions of the story, but I believe this figurine is a more canonical representation.
Zh: “是的，这个故事有好多相互矛盾的版本，但我相信这个小雕像是更加标准的表示。”

En: Did I mention that this figurine is supposed to be lucky? Always carry it with you. He grins.
Zh: “我有没有说过这个小雕像是能带来好运的？记得一直带在身上。”他咧嘴一笑。

En: Step away from the table. [Leave.]
Zh: 远离柜子。[离开。]

En: They're not all blue. These figurines also wear gold coats and caps, complimented by orange trousers. They are variously posed, wielding swords and rifles with bayonets.
Zh: 并不全是蓝色的。这些小雕像也可以穿着金色外套和帽子，配上橙色的裤子。他们的姿势各种各样，挥舞着宝剑或者带刺刀的步枪。

En: This set of soldiers isn't meant to look impressive. A few have rifles, but most of them carry pistols -- some even shovels and tall sticks.
Zh: 这套士兵并不打算给人留下深刻的印象。有的上面有步枪，但大部分都是手枪——有的甚至是铲子和码尺。

En: (Point at the blue figurines.) "What exactly are these?"
Zh: （指着蓝色的小雕像。）“这些到底是什么？”

En: (Point at the blue figurines.) "Are these royalist soldiers?"
Zh: （指着蓝色的小雕像。）“这些是皇家士兵吗？”

En: Which ones? The man peers from his glass box. "Ah! Royalist soldiers from the time of the Revolution. The uniforms are painted a bit too brightly, I suppose."
Zh: “哪个？”男人从他的玻璃亭里往外看，“啊！大革命时期的皇家士兵。我觉得制服颜色好像有点太亮了。”

En: Which ones? The man peers from his glass box. "Ah, those! Yes, they are. I find the paint job a bit gaudy, but children like bright colours indiscriminately."
Zh: “哪个？”男人从他的玻璃亭里往外看，“啊，那个啊！是的，没错。我觉得颜料有点艳俗了，不过小孩就是不分青红皂白地喜欢亮色。”

En: (Point at the figurines in rags.) "Are these even soldiers?"
Zh: （指着衣衫褴褛的小雕像。）“这些真算得上是士兵吗？”

En: You're probably talking about the revolutionaries, yes? the man behind the glass answers. "Yes -- they are soldiers. Revolutionary soldiers."
Zh: “你大概是在说革命分子，对吧？”玻璃后的男人回答到。“是的——他们是士兵。大革命士兵。”

En: I think their poverty has been exaggerated for effect. When you place them next to the royalists, it doesn't seem like they could possibly win.
Zh: “我觉得为了达到效果，有点夸大他们的贫穷了。你把他们放到皇家士兵旁边，看起来不太可能会赢的样子。”

En: It's meant to give people hope -- even *we* can do it.
Zh: “这是为了给人们带去希望——就连*我们*也能做到。”

En: The contrast is meant to be disheartening. As it ought to be.
Zh: “这种对比就是为了让人感到沮丧，按道理来说也应该是这样。”

En: Maybe.
Zh: “也许吧。”

En: I don't like either set very much, to be honest, but there are many parents among my customers.
Zh: “老实说，我也不太喜欢那一套，但是我的客人当中有很多是父母。”

En: He seems to have his own take on the conflict played out in perpetuity by these toys. Might be interesting to find out what it is...
Zh: 他似乎对这些玩具之间上演的永恒冲突有自己的看法。如果能发现到底是什么，应该会很有趣……

En: That was a very smooth salesman's grin that almost comes off as earnest. You should learn from him.
Zh: 那是个非常标准的售货员招牌笑容，感觉确实发自真心。你应该跟他好好学学。
